races.name: Ascot Racecourse Supports Horse Rangers Hampton Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Bathara
runners.spotlight: Warwick bumper winner on good ground and was then best of the rest behind a runaway winner when penalised at Ascot; 5-1 for hurdle debut at Newcastle (2m4f) and reportedly bled from the nose after pulling up; retains potential but this is no easy race in which to bounce back.
runners.comment: Bumper winner; bled from the nose when pulled up on hurdle debut (5-1)

runners.name: Gnomon
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who then won two Flat handicaps for Ralph Beckett; he's getting there over hurdles judged on his near miss at Taunton (2m, good) and this longer trip could trigger the improvement he'll need to trouble West Hill Verde.
runners.comment: This longer trip could trigger the improvement he'll need to trouble West Hill Verde
runners.owner: Andrew And Sarah Wates And Gt Racing

runners.name: The Egyptian Ginge
runners.spotlight: Has something to find on all the figures with West Hill Verde but this eight-race maiden probably ran his best race yet in a Haydock handicap (2m3f, good to soft) and the winner is an improver who has followed up; effective on good ground so should give his running.
runners.comment: 0-8 but latest second in a Haydock handicap was right up there with his best form

runners.name: Mahon Falls
runners.spotlight: Runner up in a bumper and two maiden hurdles for Willie Mullins (on good and soft) and the Cork maiden in which she was a remote third in December (final run) was dominated by good horses; while she's up to winning races for her new connections, she may have to wait for another day.
runners.comment: Runner up in a bumper and two maiden hurdles for Willie Mullins; can win races
runners.owner: E W Lewis & Katy Price Racing

races.name: David Keen Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Noahsgreatrainbow
runners.spotlight: Listed bumper runner-up who failed to win over hurdles (0-4) but went in at the first try over fences at Wincanton in December (2m4f, soft), when well on top late on to collect by 6l (5lb lower); since then he'll pulled up and finished 13l behind the winner when only three completed at Doncaster (2m3f, good); he's not crying out to be backed after those efforts but there's no depth to this.
runners.comment: Hasn't built on his winning start over fences but he's in a race here with little depth

runners.name: Sherborne
runners.spotlight: Consistent 10yo; he beat only two other finishers at Exeter (2m3f, good to soft; fine on good) and his pursuers appeared to underperform; however, the 7lb rise shouldn't be too damaging given he's performed well off similar figures in the past; solid contender.
runners.comment: Exeter race fell apart and he's up 7lb; however, has shown he can deal with this mark

runners.name: Limetree Boy
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler who is 0-15 over fences; placed a few times, as at Windsor on New Year's Day (3m, good), but lesser performances have followed and softer ground was no excuse; cheekpieces return after trying blinkers last time.
runners.comment: 0-15 over fences but placed a few times; has to rebound from two below-par efforts

runners.name: Diplomatic Ash
runners.spotlight: Won twice around Fontwell (2m3f/2m2f) during this month last year and well treated on all that; didn't appear to see out 3m last time at Kempton and the return of cheekpieces is a likely positive; goes on good ground; hard to dismiss.
runners.comment: Well in on winning form a year ago; didn't appear to stay 3m latest; cheekpieces back on

runners.name: Moroder
runners.spotlight: Ran no race in Doncaster's Grimthorpe last month (3m2f, soft; blinkers returned) and he had a good record in that race; did run quite well there the time before; unwise to focus too much on his reduced mark when clearly not the force of old, but he has returned to winning ways from out of the blue in the past, and he'll enjoy getting back on forecast good ground; earns respect.
runners.comment: Becoming hard to predict but has won out of the blue in the past; earns respect

races.name: Sodexo Live! Juvenile Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Macshadow Des Crai
runners.spotlight: Placed in French bumpers; contested useful races in his first two hurdle starts and, having travelled best, he appeared to be outstayed by Senator on soft ground at Market Rasen; could be dangerous if this surface suits him better and he has had a wind operation.
runners.comment: Outstayed by Senator last time on soft; drier ground this time and he's had a wind op

runners.name: Senator
runners.spotlight: Placed in two French Flat races; maintained his progress over hurdles with a short-priced maiden win over Macshadow Des Crai at Market Rasen (soft) and he brings an unexposed profile into handicaps; there are some concerns on the ground front.
runners.comment: Maiden winner who is progressing; there are some concerns on the ground front

runners.name: The Bellhop
runners.spotlight: 2-4 over hurdles, the wins coming on good and heavy ground at Fontwell; dropped 1lb for finishing a 3l third on handicap debut at Sandown (soft) and there was enough in that run to believe he's handicapped to win again.
runners.comment: Dual winner and enough promise in his Sandown run to believe this mark is doable

runners.name: Gallivanted
runners.spotlight: AW winner at 1m4f; made most to win at Musselburgh (good to soft) but was beaten a good way when penalised at Huntingdon, and he was about 10l behind King Al when they clashed at Ludlow in February; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Made most at Musselburgh but looks vulnerable on his two efforts either side of that

runners.name: Innamorato
runners.spotlight: Placed on the Flat and over hurdles in France; it was a quiet start for Paul Nicholls but came good in a heavy-ground maiden at Taunton (2m3f) and then had Mojito Des Mottes 4l behind when third in a slowly run race at Newbury; whether he wants good ground remains to be seen but open to improvement in a race of this nature (handicap debut).
runners.comment: Ex-French gelding who seems to be getting it together over hurdles; good going a grey area

runners.name: Mojito Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: Burdened with his French penalty when no closer than about 6l in his qualifying races and wasn't that strong in the finish in any of those; however, may well reveal a higher level of ability after a wind operation; the betting could be instructive.
runners.comment: French winner; underwhelming so far in Britain but has undergone a wind operation

runners.name: Paddockwood
runners.spotlight: Won only one of his 15 Flat races in France but won two of his four hurdle starts at Cagnes-Sur-Mer, both on very soft; it was all a bit of a culture shock on British debut at Cheltenham (100-1 for the Fred Winter) where he raced far too freely to give himself any realistic hope; reservations for now, and Ben Jones gets off to partner his stablemate.
runners.comment: Did well in France but raced far too freely on British debut in the Fred Winter

runners.name: Galactique
runners.spotlight: Like The Bellhop, both his wins have been at Fontwell (good/good to soft); however, he was well behind his stablemate when both were making their handicap debuts at Sandown and this 4yo didn't look straightforward that day.
runners.comment: Dual Fontwell winner; finished last on handicap debut and didn't look that straightforward

runners.name: Bandjo
runners.spotlight: In France he won over 1m4f and a 2m1f hurdle on very soft ground; didn't run too badly for a 150-1 chance in the Fred Winter but he's probably plenty high enough in the weights.
runners.comment: Ran okay on UK debut in the Fred Winter but he's probably too high in the weights

runners.name: King Al
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner who progressed again to edge out Talakan at Ludlow in February and, although it was a rather sobering defeat at Warwick, that was against older opposition; good ground won't bother him but others might be on kinder marks.
runners.comment: Ludlow winner; took on older horses last time; others might be on kinder marks

runners.name: Talakan
runners.spotlight: Consistent on the Flat in France where his win came over 1m6f (heavy); it's been a likeable start over hurdles, showing similar sort of form in his three defeats; closely matched with King Al on their Ludlow clash and this gelding then bumped into a promising rival at Warwick (good to soft; unraced on good); unlikely to be far away on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Consistent on the Flat and in his three defeats over hurdles; closely matched with King Al

runners.name: Munsif
runners.spotlight: Won three of his six races (at up to 1m4f) for Roger Varian and was useful; 0-4 over hurdles but bar one blip it's been a promising start; he was facing testing ground for the first time in his life when third at Naas in the race that yet again provided the winner of the Fred Winter and conditions were again slow when third in a big-field maiden last week at Navan; suspicion there's more in the locker that could be unleashed now handicapping, especially back on a drier surface; big player.
runners.comment: Useful Flat horse; plenty of promise over hurdles on ground perhaps too slow; h'cap debut

runners.name: Cayman Dancer
runners.spotlight: 1m2f/1m4f winner on the AW for previous yard; settled better than in his first two runs over hurdles when demoted from first place in a 2m soft-ground maiden at Fontwell 15 days ago; that was a step forward but this handicap debut demands another.
runners.comment: Dual Flat winner; demoted from first in a Fontwell maiden; likely to need more here

races.name: Ascot Racecourse Supports Priors Court School Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Pure Carbon
runners.spotlight: Two wins and as many seconds in his completed starts over fences; put in his place off this mark at Kempton (2m2f, good) two weeks ago but the winner is in a good place at the minute; unlikely to be far away.
runners.comment: Two wins and as many seconds in his completed starts over fences; ran well last time

runners.name: Yes Indeed
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back wins last spring (2m/2m1f; good to soft/good) on first two starts for this yard; essentially underwhelming since, albeit running at the Cheltenham Festival last time when 66-1 for the 2m4f handicap; this is clearly less demanding but he brings risks in new cheekpieces.
runners.comment: This is not so demanding as Cheltenham race last time; tries new cheekpieces

runners.name: Cobblers Boy
runners.spotlight: It's been a ropey start over fences (last four races) but he could be happy to get away from soft ground, having won his maiden hurdle on good; better can be expected but he's risky.
runners.comment: It's been a ropey start over fences; much depends on whether he needs this good ground

runners.name: Haas Boy
runners.spotlight: Sported this headgear combination for the first time when narrowly repelling Authoceltic at Newton Abbot and followed up over hurdles later in the autumn; disappointed back over fences in January but returned to form with a decisive win at Wincanton (1m7f, good to soft), beating a hat-trick seeker in the process; went up 8lb for that but clearly a contender; good ground is fine.
runners.comment: Having a good season in this headgear combination and has to rate a strong contender

runners.name: Authoceltic
runners.spotlight: Only three runs over fences but already a better chaser than hurdler; well on top to win at Wincanton in November (1m7f, good; 5lb lower) and the softer surface at Ludlow (good to soft) was a feasible excuse next time; absent since December but likely to go well.
runners.comment: Improved on good ground at Wincanton; good to soft perhaps not ideal at Ludlow latest

races.name: Barrie Wells Box4Kids Novices' Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Low Expectations
runners.spotlight: Usually ridden by James Davies and won three 2m-2m4f handicaps in a row in November/December; backward steps on his two outings since and his free-going tendency is a concern for this longer trip.
runners.comment: Backward steps on last two outings; free-going tendency is a concern for this longer trip

runners.name: Joyau Allen
runners.spotlight: Brother to high-class Envoi Allen; all starts on softer than good; heavy-ground bumper winner who has not made a serious impact in his four hurdle races (bled from the nose on first two occasions) but showed his best form when beaten about 19l at Taunton (2m3f) on last two outings; looks set to face very different ground here but the step up in trip and handicap debut could reveal him in a new light.
runners.comment: The step up in trip and handicap debut could reveal him in a new light

runners.name: Air Apparent
runners.spotlight: Four maiden/novice hurdles, best effort when 18l third of 12 at Hereford (2m4f, good) last time; raw type with plenty of the stayer about him but he's sometimes raced freely; needs a second look over new trip on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Plenty of the stayer about him but he's sometimes raced freely; new trip on handicap debut
runners.owner: A Cocks T Johnson & P Downing

runners.name: Kilworth
runners.spotlight: Showed improved form and a willing attitude to get off the mark over hurdles on handicap debut at Taunton (good to soft) in January, keeping on stoutly over an extended 3m; ran poorly when favourite at Plumpton (extended 2m4f, soft) five weeks ago, but considered on the former effort.
runners.comment: Won handicap debut at Taunton (good to soft) over extended 3m; ran poorly five weeks ago

runners.name: Glance Of Glory
runners.spotlight: Two wins over 2m4f on good ground at Worcester (maiden/handicap) in the autumn and creditable fourth at Aintree (3m, soft) in December when his jumping lacked fluency and he didn't see the trip out that well; disputed favouritism at Newbury (2m4f) last month but clipped heels and slipped up before halfway; cheekpieces go on and he needs to resume improvement, with Sean Bowen (on board for those wins) back.
runners.comment: Creditable fourth at 3m; disputed favouritism last month but clipped heels and slipped up

runners.name: A Good Yomp
runners.spotlight: Ridden throughout this season by 10lb claimer Dillon Butler; plenty of experience over hurdles and the one win, at Huntingdon (2m4f, good to soft) in November in a first-time hood which has been kept since; good third last time but that was over 2m, which casts doubt over this new trip.
runners.comment: Experienced and running well but latest start was 2m, which casts doubt over this new trip

runners.name: Kiwi Rush
runners.spotlight: Soft-ground bumper winner who showed plenty of ability on first three hurdle starts (two at about 2m4f) before a poor show on handicap debut at Wincanton (9-1) last time; needs to rebound and also to find improvement over this new trip; others have a bit less to prove.
runners.comment: Needs to rebound from poor handicap debut and also to find improvement over this new trip

runners.name: Hard Dealt
runners.spotlight: Won Worcester novice (good) last May; contested handicaps in her five starts since, twice a flop but runner-up in the other three, including when upped to 3m at Haydock (good to soft) on latest outing; considered each-way.
runners.comment: Twice a flop in her five handicaps but second in the other three, including at 3m latest

runners.name: Alan Bresil
runners.spotlight: May have started handicap life on too high a mark, as he was beaten 19l and 14l in his two handicaps (2m7f/3m); wore cheekpieces on last two outings; needs overall improvement.
runners.comment: Beaten 19l and 14l in his two handicaps (2m7f/3m); needs overall improvement

races.name: Sodexo Live! Veterans' Handicap Chase (Leg 4 Of The Stayers' Veterans' Chase Series)

runners.name: Hold Your Fort
runners.spotlight: Won at Ludlow (3m) in October and creditable fourth off this mark at Chepstow (3m2f, again good to firm) four days later, both under Callum Pritchard; recent Taunton run ended in an unseat before push came to shove but he's a big player judged on that autumn form.
runners.comment: Unseated three weeks ago but he's a big player judged on his autumn form on good to firm

runners.name: Copperhead
runners.spotlight: Unseated rider (still prominent) at Sandown in January when seeking a second success in the veterans final; battled his way back to pip Gabbys Cross for a Newbury win (3m2f) four weeks ago and, although 5lb rise takes him to his highest mark since 2022, this sterling character needs major respect.
runners.comment: Battled back to pip Gabbys Cross at Newbury; highest mark since 2022 but major respect

runners.name: Gabbys Cross
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 11yo (four races for current yard) who has not won since July 2022 but so nearly put that right in first-time cheekpieces at Newbury four weeks ago when Copperhead wrested back the advantage in the final strides; back up 4lb and Sean Bowen takes over; clearly capable of a bold show.
runners.comment: No win since 2022 but nearly put that right in cheekpieces against Copperhead last time

races.name: Colts And Fillies Club Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Afadil
runners.spotlight: 17 races and over two years since his last win, albeit still frequently competitive; big majority of his career has been at about 2m (he last went over further in October 2024) and he's never been this far.
runners.comment: 17 races since last win, albeit frequently competitive; big majority of career at about 2m

runners.name: Royal Way
runners.spotlight: Won sole start here; two years without a win after that but, with tongue-tie removed and second-time cheekpieces, he then obliged comfortably at Ludlow (2m5f, good) ten days ago; back up 6lb but has returned to serious calculations.
runners.comment: Obliged comfortably at Ludlow (2m5f, good) ten days ago; back up 6lb; considered

runners.name: Glynn Brae
runners.spotlight: Battled well for narrow win on seasonal debut at Sandown (2m4f, good to soft; six ran) in November; two disappointing efforts followed and although fourth at Chepstow (2m) on latest outing was much more like it, this step up in trip probably needs to spark overall improvement.
runners.comment: Won at Sandown (2m4f) in November; much more like it again on latest start (2m); new trip

runners.name: Dramatic Encore
runners.spotlight: Won a Huntingdon maiden (2m5f) last February and took a four-runner handicap at Plumpton (2m2f; second-time cheekpieces) 13 days ago, and good ground is probably okay, but to win this demands a career best.
runners.comment: Took a four-runner handicap at Plumpton (2m2f) 13 days ago; needs a career best

runners.name: Brave Knight
runners.spotlight: 5-10 over hurdles, including wins under Freddie Keighley at Fontwell (2m1f) and Taunton (2m3f, good) on two of his last three outings; respected even though he's never raced over this far (he worked his way to the front late on for that 2m3f win) and has been raised another 5lb.
runners.comment: 5-10 as hurdler, including 2m1f/2m3f wins on two of last three starts; unraced at this far

runners.name: Lovely Gino
runners.spotlight: French chase/hurdle winner in 2025, both at about 2m3f; pipped at Auteuil in September but brought down next time and fell when out of contention in October on latest start; reverts to hurdling for new connections on this British debut and needs a market check.
runners.comment: French chase/hurdle winner in 2025, both at about 2m3f; absent since October

runners.name: Bethpage
runners.spotlight: Progressive last spring, including 3m/3m2f wins, and good second in Kempton Listed race (2m5f) in November; nearly always races on officially good ground nowadays, so good to soft may excuse a heavy defeat in December when last seen; needs to resume her progress, though, given the career-high mark.
runners.comment: 3m/3m2f wins last spring and good second in 2m5f Kempton Listed race in November

runners.name: Whatsupwithyou
runners.spotlight: Veteran who is usually ridden these days by Elliott England, including for a 2m5f Ludlow win last February and a C&D win (4lb higher today) in December; 33-1 when tailed off at Kempton five weeks ago but no shock if he surges back to form today in search of his third C&D win.
runners.comment: C&D win (4lb higher today) in December; 33-1 when tailed off at Kempton five weeks ago

runners.name: Everyonesgame
runners.spotlight: 4-17 over hurdles, the latest win when 80-1 under Freddie Mitchell at Kempton (3m, good) in November on second start for this stable; respectable seventh of ten when 40-1 at Cheltenham (3m) in December last time but his old, markedly better form may now be out of reach.
runners.comment: Won at Kempton (3m, good) in November; respectable 7th of ten at Cheltenham in December

races.name: Download The RacedayReady App Amateur Jockeys' Handicap

runners.name: Asian Journey
runners.spotlight: 0-13 but he has been hinting at better in recent starts, including when third over 1m at Southwell 16 days ago when he had to be switched at a crucial stage; no doubt he has the ability to win races off this mark but he also has his quirks and he has hung to his left under pressure in the past, including for today's rider at Southwell in January; each-way claims again.
runners.comment: 0-13 but has more ability than his mark suggests; each-way claims at least

runners.name: King Of Speed
runners.spotlight: Won off this mark in December (8.6f, AW) and he has followed that with three respectable efforts in 2026; capable of a prominent showing.
runners.comment: Won off this mark in December (8.6f, AW) and fair efforts in defeat since; not ruled out

runners.name: Rainwater
runners.spotlight: Infrequent winner but he has run some good races in defeat over the winter, including when coming from a long way back to take second at Southwell (7f) for this rider in January; ran poorly at this meeting 12 months ago.
runners.comment: Has run well for today's rider; modest strike-rate though & others appeal more for the win

runners.name: Alazwar
runners.spotlight: Good chance on his best form last year, including a 1m2f win at Newbury (good to firm; for Brodie Hampson) off a 3lb higher mark; should be spot on for this after two runs this time around; one to consider.
runners.comment: 3lb lower than for last win and should be spot on for this after two runs this year

runners.name: Rising Force
runners.spotlight: His good record for Henry Callan includes a third-place finish in the corresponding race last year (7lb higher today); arrives in good form and has the ability to feature; the ground is the slight niggle as he has yet to run on slower than good.
runners.comment: Good record for H Callan includes a 3rd in this race in 2025; unraced on slower than good

runners.name: Star Cast
runners.spotlight: Two wins at Newcastle this year, both over 1m2f; has shown promise in her four turf runs but there is a suspicion she will need the pace to collapse back down in trip.
runners.comment: Two 1m2f AW wins this year; suspicion she will need the pace to collapse back at a mile

runners.name: Tees George
runners.spotlight: Twice finished second last summer; pleasing return to action when third of nine at Newcastle last month (7f); seems to handle most ground and he is another to consider in a wide-open handicap.
runners.comment: Promising reappearance last month (7f, AW); another one to consider back up in trip

runners.name: Four Fifty
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut at Newcastle (7f) last April; yet to make much of an impact in handicaps but he has dropped down the weights and he ran a solid race over C&D in the Leger Legends race last September; goes well after a break and he has a top amateur booked; interesting.
runners.comment: Didn't progress from debut win last spring but returns on a dangerous mark; interesting

runners.name: King Of York
runners.spotlight: Still on a realistic mark and several good AW runs to his name already this year; 0-13 on turf and 0-31 at 1m+ so he will need to break new ground to come out on top today; Simon Walker booked but others might be seeing it out more strongly.
runners.comment: 0-13 on turf and 0-31 at 1m+ so he will need to break new ground if he is to win
runners.owner: A Baker D Slack M Wilson & Partners

runners.name: Ey Up Its Jazz
runners.spotlight: 1m on good to soft ground no problem and he is feasibly weighted on his best efforts last year; should be sharper with his recent return to action under his belt and he has the assistance of a leading amateur in the saddle; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Sharper for recent reappearance; leading amateur booked; he looks one for the shortlist

runners.name: Oilisa
runners.spotlight: Too early to pigeonhole her as a mudlark but her form did take off when faced with soft and heavy going last autumn, winning at Ayr and Nottingham (both around 1m); only sixth on good ground when last seen; likely she has more to offer this year but perhaps when underfoot conditions are more demanding.
runners.comment: Progressive on deep ground last year, winning twice; capable of further progress in 2026

runners.name: Summer Evening
runners.spotlight: Chased home Rising Force at Lingfield in January (1m, AW; cheekpieces first time; ridden by Louise Chance); form dipped last time and she will need a career best today.
runners.comment: Ran well for this rider in January; form dipped last time; others look stronger

runners.name: Luminous Warrior
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark with a game effort in a 1m handicap at Kempton in December; two bitterly disappointing runs have followed and he now has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival in two runs following his breakthrough win in December (1m); risky

runners.name: Amaysmont
runners.spotlight: Drew a blank in 2025; runner-up at Wolverhampton (7f) last month but that was in a 0-55; opposable at this level.
runners.comment: Last month's AW second came in a 0-55; opposable at this level

runners.name: Woodleigh
runners.spotlight: 3lb higher than for his Redcar win last October (1m2f, good to soft); not so good on AW twice afterwards and returns from a break in a competitive race; inexperienced amateur booked.
runners.comment: 1m2f win off 3lb lower last October; returns from a break in a competitive event

runners.name: Dapper Gee Gee
runners.spotlight: Ran some good races on turf last season, including a 7f Class 4 win on good to soft at Chepstow; better than ever when readily seeing off 11 rivals at Southwell 12 weeks ago; still looks well treated up just 2lb but he has a bit more to prove over today's trip.
runners.comment: 2lb rise for smooth 7f AW win in January looks very fair; slightly more to prove at a mile

runners.name: Star Start
runners.spotlight: Three wins for today's rider; in good form last summer and he has had a wind operation (his second) since last seen; his record fresh doesn't suggest this will be his day though and it has been a long time since he ran over a trip this short.
runners.comment: Goes well for today's rider but both the trip and the 174-day absence are concerns

runners.name: Port Louis
runners.spotlight: Should appreciate the return to turf but he is rated higher in this sphere and arrives on the back of a lacklustre effort at Southwell 18 days ago; best form on good ground so drying conditions would suit.
runners.comment: Lacklustre run on AW 18 days ago; return to turf a positive but still not a solid option

runners.name: Misemerald
runners.spotlight: Won two of her first three starts but beaten in her next 21 races; down in the weights but she looks up against it in this field.
runners.comment: Yet to win a handicap and this looks plenty competitive enough back from a break

runners.name: Kinross Claret
runners.spotlight: Some promise but recorded modest RPRs in three novice/maiden runs at up to 1m; open to improvement now handicapping but this big-field scenario will present a very different test; absent 408 days.
runners.comment: Unexposed handicap debutant but he has a 408-day absence to contend with

runners.name: Cartwheel
runners.spotlight: Kicked off her 2025 campaign in the Group 3 Nell Gwyn Stakes and didn't run too badly; subsequent handicap efforts have been poor, beating just two rivals home in five attempts; dropped 23lb but doesn't look ready to capitalise just yet.
runners.comment: Tumbled down the weights but with good reason; plenty to prove back on turf

runners.name: Thanks Angel
runners.spotlight: Unexposed filly who ran a fine race over 7f here on her 2yo debut; didn't feature in two handicap runs last autumn but the ground was soft on both occasions and there were other mitigating factors too (6f too sharp at Hamilton and poor draw at Pontefract); too soon to write her off and drying ground would be beneficial.
runners.comment: Yet to build on debut promise but excuses for handicap defeats; too soon to write her off

races.name: Stonehouse Travel Agents Barnsley Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: King Of Earth
runners.spotlight: Kingman half-brother to QEII winner King Of Change; entered in the 2,000 Guineas; promising second, clear of remainder, behind a fellow newcomer in autumn contest at Newbury (1m, soft); leading contender, provided the drop in distance doesn't backfire.
runners.comment: Promising second, clear of remainder, in 1m autumn contest at Newbury; leading contender

runners.name: Naval Light
runners.spotlight: 360,000gns breeze-up purchase who showed plenty of ability in notable 2yo races over 5f-7f on good/good to firm, including at Royal Ascot; respectable third when joint-favourite for the Convivial Maiden at York on final outing; gelded since; big player on form.
runners.comment: Showed plenty of ability in notable 2yo races; gelded since Convivial effort; big player

runners.name: Im Just Ken
runners.spotlight: Both starts over 7f on AW, modest third at Wolverhampton then fifth of eight (better form) at Kempton; needs further improvement for the switch to turf and may be more interesting in handicaps shortly.
runners.comment: Turf debutant whose AW efforts suggest he'll be more interesting in handicaps

runners.name: The Golden Snitch
runners.spotlight: 3,000euros yearling; Galileo Gold half-brother to Italian 7.5f scorer Il Dittatore; dam unplaced at 6f-1m (RPR 55), half-sister to minor winners up to 1m; best watched on debut.
runners.comment: 3,000euros yearling; by Galileo Gold; best watched on debut

runners.name: Record Day
runners.spotlight: Â£3,000 yearling; Showcasing half-brother to 7f winner Monsieur Bondy (RPR 82), out of well-related 6f/7f AW 2yo scorer (75); one of two newcomers for his connections; market instructive.
runners.comment: Â£3,000 yearling; by Showcasing; one of two newcomers for his connections

runners.name: Viper
runners.spotlight: Â£1,000 yearling; by Zoustar; dam unraced sister to Group-placed Czech/Slovak 1m-1m1f scorer Tamarind Cove, out of Irish 1,000 Guineas winner Saoire; connections also run Record Day; check the betting.
runners.comment: Â£1,000 yearling; by Zoustar; connections also run Record Day

runners.name: Simon Pierre
runners.spotlight: 72,000euros 2yo; Zelzal half-brother to three winners, notably Hopeful (French/Australian 7.5f-1m4f including 2yo Group 3; RPR 109); dam 9.5f scorer; pitched into fairly deep waters on debut.
runners.comment: 72,000euros 2yo; by Zelzal; pitched into fairly deep waters on debut

runners.name: Firewalker
runners.spotlight: Nicely bred colt who cost 360,000gns as a foal and holds a 2,000 Guineas entry; unable to justify very short odds in Southwell event (6f, AW) in December but it was a solid effort in splitting subsequent scorers and this extra furlong may suit better; notable contender with progress on the cards.
runners.comment: Nicely bred; solid second, splitting subsequent winners, at Southwell; notable contender

runners.name: Halla Bil Zain
runners.spotlight: Largely consistent maiden (0-6) last term for Owen Burrows, latterly in nurseries off marks in the low 70s; sold for 25,000gns and gelded since last run; faces a stiffer assignment in this useful novice event.
runners.comment: Largely consistent maiden last term for Owen Burrows but this is a stiff assignment

runners.name: Brave Hunter
runners.spotlight: Absent since facing a difficult task and finishing eighth of nine in the Chesham at Royal Ascot; displayed some promise with third of six at Yarmouth (7f) and may show improvement back down in class; holds a (currently optimistic) 2,000 Guineas entry.
runners.comment: Absent since facing a difficult task at Royal Ascot; displayed some promise at Yarmouth

runners.name: Langstone
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and too green to do himself justice in 6f maiden at Leicester, recording a modest RPR; gelded since; may do better but has plenty to find on bare form.
runners.comment: Too green to do himself justice in Leicester maiden, recording a modest RPR

runners.name: Princess Coco
runners.spotlight: Brings easily the worst form having achieved little in 6f novice events, earning a mark of 30; would need a huge step forward even in the handicap sphere.
runners.comment: Brings easily the worst form

races.name: William Hill Epic Boosts Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Just Queen High
runners.spotlight: Record is 2-5, both wins over 6f at Nottingham (good/heavy); took well to blinkers when last seen; shapes as if she'll be as effective back at 7f; respected with further progress possible.
runners.comment: Record is 2-5; took well to blinkers when last seen; open to further improvement

runners.name: Beauty Choice
runners.spotlight: Hindered by traffic issues last time (AW); the 9yo hasn't won since 2024 but is nicely weighted, especially if Lewis Chalkley's 7lb claim is taken into account.
runners.comment: Met traffic issues last time; hasn't won since 2024 but is nicely weighted

runners.name: Golden Strike
runners.spotlight: Record of 122, all over 7f on AW, since wearing visor; most recently bumped into thriving rival who was on the second leg of a four-timer; dual winner on turf; solid claims in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Record of 122, all on AW, since wearing visor; dual turf winner; solid contender

runners.name: Quest For Fun
runners.spotlight: Still capable of fairly useful form last year; however, has a modest strike-rate since 2023 and seems unlikely to improve it on seasonal debut, having never won when fresh.
runners.comment: Reappearance isn't the time to catch him; has never won when fresh

runners.name: Packetofbiscuits
runners.spotlight: May remain competitive provided his recent Southwell AW form is transferred back to turf; however, his record in this sphere, which includes a seller win, is less convincing; presents a dilemma.
runners.comment: Recent AW form puts him in the picture; turf record is less convincing; presents a dilemma
runners.owner: A Pot To Pete In Syndicate

runners.name: An Outlaws Grace
runners.spotlight: Last of seven at Lingfield (6f, AW) on reappearance, taking record since debut win to 0-8; drops in class but has to concede weight all round and remains unproven over 7f.
runners.comment: Record of 0-8 since debut win; drops in class but is still unproven beyond 6f

runners.name: Itsneverjustone
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back wins at Ffos Las and Galway in June/July proved the highlight of his 2025 campaign; won't peak until the summer if this term goes the same way.
runners.comment: Won't peak until the summer if this term goes the same way as last season
runners.owner: Ian OConnor & The Fox And Hounds

runners.name: Grabajabba
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner, most recently in February; two attempts on turf include a good effort (close third off 1lb higher) in a division of this contest 12 months ago; again has place claims.
runners.comment: Ran well (third off 1lb higher) in a division of this contest 12 months ago; place claims

runners.name: Tactical Plan
runners.spotlight: Successful off 2lb higher as a 2yo; ended his 2025 season with sound efforts over C&D (fourth of 15) and at Redcar (head second); possibilities assuming he returns to action in similar form.
runners.comment: Ended his 2025 season with sound efforts over C&D and at Redcar; possibilities

runners.name: Alfa Whiteburd
runners.spotlight: Far from solid on his form for current yard, raced mostly over 6f and yet to win on turf; enough to prove in this scenario; had wind surgery (his second) since last run.
runners.comment: Raced mainly over 6f and yet to win on turf; opposed

runners.name: Nakaaha
runners.spotlight: Similar type to It'sneverjustone, with a summer double (1m) the highlight of her 2025 campaign; something to prove back over 7f and may be one for later on.
runners.comment: Dual 1m winner who has something to prove back over 7f

runners.name: Backspin
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly on AW for Richard Hughes, winning once (6f); sold for 14,000gns since last run; something to prove back on turf but market support should be noted.
runners.comment: AW scorer for Richard Hughes; something to prove back on turf; stable debut

runners.name: Jimmy Speaking
runners.spotlight: Ended last season on a low note but holds a fighting chance provided he's resurgent on reappearance; has recorded his turf wins off current mark and just 1lb lower.
runners.comment: Fighting chance off current mark provided he's resurgent on reappearance

runners.name: Green Pursuit
runners.spotlight: Absent since down the field at Doncaster (6f) five months ago on first run since rejoining Seb Spencer; perhaps best watched unless the market speaks positively.
runners.comment: Absent since finishing down the field at Doncaster five months ago

runners.name: Victory Sound
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner for previous yard; has finished last in all four starts for new stable; opposed unless the betting suggests a revival is likely.
runners.comment: Has finished last in all four starts for new stable

runners.name: Under The Twilight
runners.spotlight: Has gained all turf wins at Salisbury and Windsor but appears effective at Doncaster, having finished 2l third off 4lb higher in this race on reappearance last year; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Finished 2l third off 4lb higher in this race on reappearance last year; each-way shout

races.name: William Hill Epic Boosts Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Epictetus
runners.spotlight: Group 3 winner (1m, soft) for previous yard; 0-14 for current stable but went down by only a neck at Chelmsford (AW) most recently and that was only his second attempt over 7f; in the mix.
runners.comment: 0-14 for current stable but went close on AW last time and is still unexposed at 7f

runners.name: Captain Parma
runners.spotlight: Consistent AW handicapper who ran creditably last time when bidding for a Chelmsford double (7f); can remain competitive provided the form is transferred back to turf (second attempt).
runners.comment: Consistent on AW; can remain competitive provided form is transferred back to turf

runners.name: Miletus
runners.spotlight: Novice winner who is 0-9 in handicaps but has started favourite in both runs (1m, AW) for new yard and the way he shaped last time suggests this return to 7f is worth exploring; tongue-tie fitted; possibilities.
runners.comment: 0-9 in handicaps but this return to 7f is worth exploring on third start for new yard

runners.name: Folkene
runners.spotlight: Won well at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) on debut for current stable but has failed to back up the form in three subsequent runs; perhaps best watched unless the betting foretells a turnaround.
runners.comment: Perhaps best watched unless the betting foretells a turnaround

runners.name: Forever My Prince
runners.spotlight: Absent since taking advantage of a good chance at the weights in Leicester claimer (7f, soft) five months ago on final start for Eve Johnson Houghton; returns to a tougher scenario.
runners.comment: Won claimer on final start for Eve Johnson Houghton; returns to a tougher scenario

runners.name: Albeseeingyer
runners.spotlight: Has become inconsistent but her form over C&D features a success in spring 2023 and fourth-place finish on reappearance last year; could go well off a workable mark.
runners.comment: Form over C&D features a win in 2023 and fourth-place finish on 2025 reappearance

runners.name: Melvin Udall
runners.spotlight: Ran well in Yarmouth handicap when bidding to follow up his Redcar novice win (both 7f); subsequently sold out of Ed Walker's yard for 20,000gns; interesting contender, being unexposed.
runners.comment: Ran well on final outing for Ed Walker; still unexposed; interesting contender
runners.owner: K Mills J Brown S Barley

runners.name: War Howl
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-1 at Doncaster, namely the first leg of a turf double last summer; always towards rear in two AW races this year for new stable but may prove resurgent back in this sphere.
runners.comment: 1-1 at Doncaster, namely the first leg of a turf double last summer; may revive
runners.owner: Future Champions Racing Syn & Ben Haslam

runners.name: Shazani
runners.spotlight: Showed his best 2025 form at Doncaster, winning the Leger Legends race (1m) in September and second of 15 in handicap (7f) in October; those performances put him in the picture.
runners.comment: Showed his best 2025 form at Doncaster, winning the Legends race and second in handicap

runners.name: Singoura
runners.spotlight: AW novice winner who is 0-6 in handicaps but registered her best efforts in two attempts over C&D; nicely weighted assuming the return to this scenario has a reviving effect.
runners.comment: 0-6 in handicaps but registered her best efforts in two attempts over C&D

runners.name: Zarathos
runners.spotlight: Broadly consistent and has the ability to take a serious hand but is a frustrating maiden who has failed to justify favouritism several times; remains to be seen whether 2026 is going to pan out differently for him.
runners.comment: Broadly consistent and has the ability to take a serious hand but is a frustrating maiden
runners.owner: J Sarkar D Sarkar O Sarkar & D Sarkar

runners.name: Red Mirage
runners.spotlight: Doesn't have time on his side, being the oldest contender in this field; however, looks interesting back at Doncaster, being 1-1 here (win off 1lb lower last July); Harry Vigors takes off a useful 7lb.
runners.comment: The oldest contender in this field but is interesting back at Doncaster, being 1-1 here

runners.name: Capital Guarantee
runners.spotlight: Seems ideally suited by good ground or firmer; successful three times last season, including on reappearance and off current mark; those factors make him one to bear in mind.
runners.comment: Successful three times last term, including on reappearance and off current mark

runners.name: Stanage
runners.spotlight: Only 1-12 for current yard but gained the success off 7lb higher in a division of this contest on reappearance last year (good ground); great chance assuming he starts this season in similar form.
runners.comment: Defied a 7lb higher mark in a division of this race on reappearance last year; good chance

runners.name: Another Investment
runners.spotlight: Has become unreliable but is well treated on his standout performance of 2025, namely a comfortable win off 1lb higher at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) in June; scored over 7f in 2023.
runners.comment: Well treated on his standout performance of 2025, namely a win at Doncaster in June

races.name: William Hill NRMB On The Grand National Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Port Of London
runners.spotlight: His only turf race was debut last July when tailed off on soft ground; handicap debut last October was a blip but he did well in his other three AW races, gelded before comfortably making all with second-time cheekpieces in a ten-runner handicap at Lingfield (1m2f) in December when last seen; up 6lb but could easily have more to give.
runners.comment: Not proven on turf; comfortably made all at Lingfield (1m2f) in December in latest AW race

runners.name: Say What You See
runners.spotlight: Last five races in 2025 saw him first past the post three times and second twice; on final start he finished well on top over C&D (good to soft) but was disqualified for a banned substance; only 2lb higher today (from 2lb out of the handicap) and makes plenty of appeal if returning in a similar vein for this first start since September.
runners.comment: Well on top over C&D final 3yo run but disqualified for banned substance; 2lb higher today

runners.name: Castle Stuart
runners.spotlight: 2-6 for Karl Burke before sold for 165,000gns last July; ran well to take minor honours over 1m2f at York in two of his four subsequent starts, while 1m4f on heavy seemed to find him out in the November Handicap here on latest; can go well if sufficiently tuned up for this reappearance.
runners.comment: 1m4f on heavy was too much last November; can go well if sufficiently tuned up for return

runners.name: Harvey
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful turf winner in France as a juvenile but ran just twice last year; hooded for his two AW races for new stable this year, with an excellent third of 11 from towards rear at Kempton (1m) last time; that puts him on the shortlist despite him having something to prove over this far.
runners.comment: Ex-French; excellent third of 11 from towards rear at Kempton (1m) on latest outing
runners.owner: Miss E Nolan And Paul Hancock

runners.name: Spioradalta
runners.spotlight: Five wins last year at about 1m1f/1m2f on varying ground (one courtesy of the stewards); thereabouts if he starts this term where he left off.
runners.comment: 5 wins last year at about 1m1f/1m2f; thereabouts if he starts this term where he left off

runners.name: Knights Gold
runners.spotlight: Five handicap wins in a productive 2025; probably needed his reappearance at 50-1 three weeks ago but this is his first run beyond 8.6f and, even with a hood returning, there's a question of whether he will settle.
runners.comment: Five wins last term; probably needed his reappearance but this is first run beyond 8.6f

runners.name: Sunriseontheboyne
runners.spotlight: Six wins from his 28 races in Ireland during 2024 and 2025, one at 1m2f (last May) but he was usually seen over shorter; his two races for new yard this year were at 7f, not showing that much but a tongue-tie (worn on last four Irish starts) returns and he's back up in trip.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; not much over 7f for new yard but a tongue-tie returns and he's back up in trip
runners.owner: Future Champions Racing Syn & Ben Haslam

runners.name: Per Contra
runners.spotlight: Pretty reliable last season, including when dropped back to this sort of trip in August/September, but he seemingly needed his first two runs; chance if he returns at his best this time.
runners.comment: Pretty reliable last season but he seemingly needed his first two runs in that campaign

runners.name: Ashnak
runners.spotlight: Won at Chantilly (1m1f, heavy) on second start as 2yo, followed by minor honours at up to 1m4f all three outings last spring; left Aga Khan Studs for 35,000euros last July and the market may well guide on this first run since.
runners.comment: Left Aga Khan Studs for 35,000euros last July; market may well guide on first run since

runners.name: Spoken Truth
runners.spotlight: Trained for the previous three seasons by Dermot Weld, winning a maiden at Cork (1m2f, soft) in April 2024 from nine starts; behind last autumn on last two outings, which preceded a 32,000gns sale and gelding operation; down the weights and has an attractive mark for new connections to work with if they can spark a revival.
runners.comment: 32,000gns buy from Dermot Weld's; attractive mark for new connections granted a revival

runners.name: Have Secret
runners.spotlight: Below form in this race last year but C&D second in October was one of his mostly creditable efforts afterwards; competitive if he returns on song, as he did in 2023 and 2024; stable also runs Castle Stuart.
runners.comment: Below form in this race last year but C&D second in October; stable runs two

runners.name: Atherstone Warrior
runners.spotlight: All three wins on heavy or soft going and his career surged forward on last three starts of 2025 when he scored over 1m2f at Sandown and Newbury before his third of 22 off this mark over 1m4f in the November Handicap here; still interesting off this mark and returning to 1m2f looks fine but there's a question of whether the ground will be soft enough.
runners.comment: 3rd in 1m4f November Handicap here; 1m2f looks fine; will the ground will be soft enough?

runners.name: Too Soon
runners.spotlight: Heavy-ground 2yo winner who came up short in various, mostly warm events last season; tried blinkers over 1m4f, 1m2f and 1m6f on last three outings, which are removed today but he has now been gelded; others preferred.
runners.comment: Heavy-ground 2yo winner who came up short in various, mostly warm events last term; gelded

races.name: William Hill Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap

runners.name: Only In Manila
runners.spotlight: Disappointing in retained cheekpieces last time when making handicap debut and bidding for a Kempton AW double (1m3f); chance partly depends on how well he responds to new headgear.
runners.comment: Disappointing last time when bidding for Kempton AW double; goes in new headgear

runners.name: Morbeh
runners.spotlight: Son of Dubawi, has fared better since being gelded, tidily justifying favouritism at Lingfield (1m2f, AW; handicap debut) last time; warrants respect with further improvement plausible.
runners.comment: Won tidily at Lingfield (AW; handicap debut) last time; further improvement is plausible

runners.name: Dryburgh
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden; perhaps needed the run at Kempton (1m) on stable/seasonal debut; however, chance of winning depends on the effects of the new trip and first-time hood.
runners.comment: Chance of winning depends on the effects of new trip and first-time hood

runners.name: Kelly Burn
runners.spotlight: Fared better with hood fitted in latest AW start (8.6f); may take another step forward now handicapping with the headgear retained; new trip looks likely to suit; one to consider.
runners.comment: May take another step forward now handicapping with hood retained; one to consider

runners.name: Assaranca
runners.spotlight: Made all at Nottingham (1m) in last handicap attempt but has raced nine times already, making her the busiest contender in this field and the one with seemingly the least potential.
runners.comment: Made all in last handicap attempt but others have greater room for progress

runners.name: Siam Ruby
runners.spotlight: Both starts on AW, eye-catching fourth at Chelmsford (1m) then making all to beat two rivals at Lingfield (1m2f); still open to progress and looks interesting on handicap/turf debut.
runners.comment: Made all at Lingfield in the latest of two AW starts; still open to progress; interesting

runners.name: Arc Ole Ole
runners.spotlight: Scored comfortably at Bath (soft) on the second of two October outings and could do well in handicaps this season; bred to be well suited by this longer distance; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Scored comfortably at Bath when last seen and could do well in handicaps this season

runners.name: Steel Fixer
runners.spotlight: Has weak claims on 2yo form but this switch to handicap level, receiving plenty of weight all round, may prompt a bigger effort; check the market signals for further guidance.
runners.comment: Has weak claims on 2yo form but this handicap scenario may prompt a bigger effort

races.name: William Hill Each Way Extra Handicap

runners.name: Irish Nectar
runners.spotlight: Weighted to reverse October's Pontefract form with Fine Interview; best run for new stable came when second at Kempton last month; raced too freely at Newcastle since; cheekpieces replace blinkers.
runners.comment: Well weighted on best 2025 form; good run last month but less good 17 days ago

runners.name: Fine Interview
runners.spotlight: Close third behind Dark Thirty in this race 12 months ago; found some progress when cheekpieces were added in October, winning back-to-back handicaps at Pontefract and over C&D (soft; Korker second); perhaps found the heavy ground too much for him when last seen in November; can go well fresh and may find further progress this year; high on the list.
runners.comment: Below par (heavy) when last seen but otherwise has fine C&D record; 3rd in this last year

runners.name: Lord Bertie
runners.spotlight: Hasn't fulfilled the huge promise of his early form for William Haggas but he retains a good chunk of ability and enjoyed a resurgence last autumn, notably when a comfortable winner over C&D (soft) in October; disappointed when last seen but he should be in for another good year; back from a break is perhaps not the time to catch him but market should give clues.
runners.comment: Good win over C&D last October (soft); still on a fair mark; market useful after a break

runners.name: Territorial Knight
runners.spotlight: Strong stayer at the trip and he powered home to win over C&D last July (Class 4; 5lb lower mark than today's); followed that with good runs at Carlisle and Ayr (Bronze Cup); Kevin Ryan has had two winners and two placed horses from ten runners in the race since 2016; still has low mileage.
runners.comment: C&D win last July was followed by two solid efforts; still has low mileage; interesting

runners.name: Physique
runners.spotlight: Shaped well when best of the near-side group in this race last year, finishing fourth of the 19 runners (6f, good); burned a few fingers afterwards, often running well but unable to put his head in front; returns from a break on a dangerous mark (back with his 2024 trainer, having been with Pam Sly and Hayley Burton) and no doubt he will have his supporters again.
runners.comment: Ran well from a tough draw in this race last year; expensive to follow since; good mark

runners.name: Valley Ofthe Kings
runners.spotlight: Promise on turf, including over C&D, and he won twice on AW in late 2025; this race looks too competitive though.
runners.comment: Promise over C&D and won twice on AW last year; this race looks too competitive though

runners.name: Kodiac Thriller
runners.spotlight: Prominent-racer; did well for this yard last year, winning at Thirsk in May and running several good races in defeat, notably when best of the stands' rail group in the Great St Wilfrid at Ripon; 1lb lower today and a big run could be forthcoming.
runners.comment: Did well for this yard in 2025; returns on a fair mark and should make a bold bid

runners.name: Baldomero
runners.spotlight: 2-4 at Doncaster; ended 2025 in rude health on the AW, winning handicaps at Southwell (6f), Wolverhampton and Lingfield (both 5f); his form has dipped slightly on his last two starts but it's not out of the question that this return to Doncaster will pique his interest.
runners.comment: Two-time course winner; ended 2025 strongly; could revive back at this venue

runners.name: Sixtygeesbaby
runners.spotlight: Impressed at Newmarket on second 2yo start (6f, good) and it was a strong C&D novice in which he defied a penalty on reappearance last April (good to firm); not an instant hit in handicaps but he rounded off his 3yo campaign with a close second at Newmarket in September (6f, good; solid form and good Topspeed figure); sold out of Tom Clover's for 50,000gns one month later; unraced on slower than good but remains a sprinter of some interest and the betting should be revealing for a yard with three representatives.
runners.comment: Unexposed C&D winner; ground query but otherwise lots to like on his stable debut

runners.name: Woven
runners.spotlight: Running in this race for the fourth time, having finished second in 2022 and 2024 and ninth last year; can go well fresh and he retained a good chunk of ability last year; should be arriving on the scene late.
runners.comment: Second in this race in 2022 and 2024; now a 10yo but likely to be running on late
runners.owner: John Sagar And Dunham Trading Ltd

runners.name: It Just Takes Time
runners.spotlight: Prominent-racer; made all in a 7.4f handicap last September but beaten twice off this mark afterwards; mixed record when fresh and others are more appealing.
runners.comment: Not much juice in his current mark and he has a mixed record when fresh

runners.name: Korker
runners.spotlight: No win since October 2023 but this admirable 7yo regularly acquits himself with credit, including when a fine second to Fine Interview over C&D last October (soft ground; off this mark); first time out is probably not the time to catch him though and his tendency to blow the start means he will always come with risks attached.
runners.comment: Slow-starter; on a dangerous mark and conditions no problem; likely to come on for this
runners.owner: Fine Claret RacingMrs Burke & C Wood

runners.name: Big Cyril
runners.spotlight: Useful form as a 2yo but struggled in his three runs last season; sold out of Alice Haynes's stable for 22,000gns last October; likely to come good for new connections off a reduced mark but this looks a pretty stiff task after eight months away.
runners.comment: Starts out for new yard off reduced mark; type to come good again sooner rather than later

runners.name: Arctic Summer
runners.spotlight: 6f winner in France as a 2yo; found life tough in four sprint handicap runs for Gemma Tutty last year, showing her best form when third of eight at Pontefract on the second occasion; may need more help from the handicapper.
runners.comment: 6f winner in France as a 2yo; four runs for her current stable need leaving behind

runners.name: Northern Spirit
runners.spotlight: Usually slowly away but his refusal to race at Newcastle in January was a first; won't mind getting back on the grass and he is proven in big-field handicaps; could go well at a price.
runners.comment: Return to turf may help and big-field handicaps no issue; refused to race in January
runners.owner: Steel A Win & Spring Cottage Syndicate 3

runners.name: Orne
runners.spotlight: Prominent-racer; good run when fourth on soft ground at Pontefract last October (Fine Interview first and Irish Nectar second) but failed to build on it twice afterwards; can win a sprint handicap off this mark but possibly when faced with more testing ground.
runners.comment: Slipped to a good mark but he has other pace to deal with and may prefer slower ground

runners.name: Glenfinnan
runners.spotlight: Poorly drawn and met trouble when finishing in midfield in this race 12 months ago; essentially disappointing afterwards, despite some assistance from the handicapper; may come on for this first start since November.
runners.comment: Starts the year on a fair mark but his 2025 campaign was essentially disappointing

runners.name: Jungle Drums
runners.spotlight: Sprung an 80-1 surprise at Chelmsford in October (6f; dead-heated) but failed to back it up at Southwell two weeks later; others look safer.
runners.comment: Popped up at 80-1 on AW last October but less good next time; others look safer
runners.owner: Jim And Claire Ltd And Jon & Kay Collins

runners.name: Dark Thirty
runners.spotlight: Prominent-racer for Richard Hannon, winning six times; three of those wins came on his seasonal reappearance, including in this race 12 months ago, and he is feasibly weighted on this first run for the Quinns (sold for 25,000gns last October); one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Fine record fresh, including winning this race in 2025; sold 25,000gns since latest; solid

runners.name: Akkadian Thunder
runners.spotlight: His last two wins have come at this track (7f); returns from a 141-day absence on a good mark, 6lb lower than when second at Royal Ascot last June (7f, good to firm); unplaced in each of his three seasonal debuts but he would have gone close at Leicester on last spring's return had he seen any daylight; the owners also run Sixtygeesbaby, while this 6yo is also one of three runners for David O'Meara.
runners.comment: Talented and well handicapped; good course record but yet to win over 6f; yard runs three

races.name: William Hill Price Boosts Every Day Handicap

runners.name: Charlie Mason
runners.spotlight: Three wins in 2025, the latest at this track (6f, good) in September when 3lb lower; several good runs in defeat since and he is a leading contender with this likely to be run to suit.
runners.comment: 6f win here in September; several good runs in defeat since; major player

runners.name: Ay Gee Ell
runners.spotlight: Transformed by the addition of a visor when running away with a 5f handicap at Southwell in December; he has run creditably in defeat since but he will need to raise his game now back on turf.
runners.comment: Easy win in first visor in December; held form since but needs extra to defy this mark

runners.name: Thornaby Pearl
runners.spotlight: Has gone well fresh in the past, as when winning a division of this race in 2024 (soft ground); only 2lb higher than when winning at Hamilton last August (first run after wind surgery); failed to beat a rival in his two subsequent runs; unlikely to find it easy dominating in this field.
runners.comment: Won a division of this race in 2024; ended 2025 with two poor runs; others look stronger

runners.name: Our Absent Friends
runners.spotlight: Seven wins, the latest at Newcastle in December off a 6lb lower mark; good second off 1lb lower back at Newcastle 24 days ago; should give his running but others may prove better treated.
runners.comment: Good second at Newcastle 24 days ago; might find one or two better treated

runners.name: I Need Your Love
runners.spotlight: Two good second-place finishes this year but he ran no sort of race at Southwell 19 days ago; won't mind getting back on the turf but risky after the last run.
runners.comment: 2nd on AW in January/February but ran a lacklustre race 19 days ago; quick revival needed

runners.name: The Fixer
runners.spotlight: Speedy sort; yet to strike for current stable; ran two good races at Southwell in late 2025 but his last three starts leave him with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Placed twice at Southwell in late 2025 but well short of that level on last three starts

runners.name: Glory Hyde
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f handicap at Catterick off this mark last May; should be sharper with her recent reappearance behind her, a luxury she didn't have when seventh in this race last year.
runners.comment: 7th in this 12 months ago but has the benefit of a recent run this time round; considered

runners.name: Emperor Caradoc
runners.spotlight: Two 6f wins on fast ground last season; made a pleasing return to action at Newcastle 17 days ago and he is one to take seriously despite dropping in distance.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins last year; promising return to action at Newcastle 17 days ago; contender

runners.name: Juan Les Pins
runners.spotlight: Ended a long losing run when narrowly landing a 5f handicap at Newcastle ten days ago; still has considerable handicapping scope and this should be run to suit, so he can't be discounted, for all that slower than good ground would be a concern.
runners.comment: Ended losing run with game win at Newcastle ten days ago; slower than good ground a worry

runners.name: Thunder Star
runners.spotlight: Fit from the AW when winning this race 12 months ago (good ground) off a 5lb lower mark; mixed bag afterwards and it looks a stronger contest this time around.
runners.comment: Won this race off 5lb lower last year when fit from AW; back from a break this time round

runners.name: Impressor
runners.spotlight: Good to soft ground no problem for him but he's happiest when the mud is flying; his record fresh doesn't suggest this will be his day.
runners.comment: No great record fresh and he is happiest when the mud is flying

runners.name: Vince LAmour
runners.spotlight: Ran well in defeat on his seasonal return in each of his two seasons of handicapping and he kicks off this campaign 8lb lower than last spring; cheekpieces replace blinkers today; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Starts the year on a handy mark and has gone well fresh before; new headgear tried today

runners.name: Fuji Mountain
runners.spotlight: Won twice at Chester as a 2yo, recording an RPR of 88 on the second occasion; the handicapper was firmly in charge last year but he did manage a close third at Redcar in October; has a five-month absence to contend with but appeals as the type to exploit his mark sooner rather than later.
runners.comment: Useful as 2yo but struggled with the handicapper last year; could rebound as a 4yo

runners.name: My Mate Kev
runners.spotlight: Hit hard by the handicapper for his comfortable Newmarket win last May (6f, good) and he found it tough thereafter; a consequence of that is that he returns off a dangerous mark and dropping to 5f could be of benefit; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Struggled after his ready Newmarket win last May (6f); down in weights for reappearance

runners.name: Yes Im Mali
runners.spotlight: Three wins for Richard Fahey; close third at Newcastle (6f) when last seen in October; sold for 22,000gns soon afterwards; goes without headgear on stable debut.
runners.comment: Three wins for R Fahey; ran well over 6f when last seen; no headgear for stable debut

runners.name: J Street
runners.spotlight: Stayed on for third (placed second) in a 5f handicap at Wolverhampton in October on her final outing for Ivan Furtado; had excuses at Newcastle on her stable debut in November; not discounted.
runners.comment: Not on a bad mark on even last year's best; had excuses on her stable debut; not ruled out

runners.name: Spoof
runners.spotlight: Veteran with 11 wins but the most recent was back in October 2023; starts this campaign off a reduced mark but others look safer.
runners.comment: Veteran on a losing run; down in the weights but others look safer

runners.name: Harrys Halo
runners.spotlight: Has had only one run over 5f and it was in this race in 2024 when he finished a well-beaten 2-1 favourite; quiet in a light campaign in 2025; others look stronger.
runners.comment: Four 6f wins but his only 5f run came when disappointing in this race in 2024
runners.owner: Jones Keane Kryswicki & Kf Racing Club

races.name: Tote Every Day's A Money Back Day Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: He Cant Dance
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner has been a bit underwhelming in five hurdling starts but has never finished outside the top three; ran on well to take third at Navan in January (2m6f, soft to heavy) and distant third in a Leopardstown Listed handicap last time (3m, heavy); new heagear combination tried and this looks like a good opportunity to bag a win with rider reducing his winner's penalty burden.
runners.comment: Underwhelming over hurdles but placed in all five runs; this looks a nice opportunity

runners.name: Gunnery Sergeant
runners.spotlight: No wins in two points; best of three maiden hurdle starts came at Fairyhouse last time when keeping on for the runner-up spot (2m4f, heavy) in a very weak contest; a tongue tie is added to the previously worn cheekpieces; stablemate He Can't Dance has better claims with Josh Williamson reducing his winner's penalty burden.
runners.comment: Runner-up last time in a very weak m'den; stablemate has better claims

runners.name: Kashchei
runners.spotlight: Moderate form for Declan Queally; has continued in a similar vein of form for new yard, fifth of seven at Punchestown last time; cheekpieces go on but others preferred.
runners.comment: Moderate form to date; fifth of seven at Punchestown last time; others preferred

runners.name: Lilac Lady
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden; decent effort on handicap hurdle debut at Sligo when last seen in October, finishing a 6.75l third (2m2f, good to yielding); may need this and needs big career best to get involved.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose m'den; decent third on h'cap hurdle debut; needs big career best to feature

runners.name: Robertjames
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; half-brother to winners Imperial Mag (useful French 2m2f dual Listed hurdles winner) and Not Negotiable (bumper); dam unraced half-sister to 14.7f AQPS Flat/2m3f hurdle/2m5f chase winner Ahquecoucou, from family of German Derby winner Kamsin; hooded on debut but stable first-string, so looks like a big contender on debut.
runners.comment: Half-brother to French dual-Listed hurdles winner; stable first string and key player

runners.name: Love Safari
runners.spotlight: Flat maiden makes his hurdling/stable debut back from a 520-day absence; some decent efforts in defeat in 2024 but best watched for now; first run since being gelded.
runners.comment: Flat m'den makes his hurdling/stable debut back from a 520-day absence; best watched

runners.name: Clone Cross
runners.spotlight: Best of five point-to-point runs was when a 12l third at Loughrea when last seen in October; vast improvement needed on rules' debut.
runners.comment: Moderate point form; vast improvement needed on rules' debut

runners.name: Microscope
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in all four points; not much to recommend him based on four runs under rules' either.
runners.comment: PU in all four points; not much to recommend him based on four runs under rules' either

runners.name: Carniny
runners.spotlight: Didn't cut much ice in either point start; always in rear when pulled up at Navan; ruled out.
runners.comment: Didn't cut much ice in points; PU at Navan; ruled out

runners.name: The Poets Way
runners.spotlight: Always behind when pulled up at Punchestown (2m3f, heavy) on debut; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Always behind when pulled up at Punchestown on debut; hard to fancy

runners.name: Papagei
runners.spotlight: Three-time British Flat winner for James Ferguson; no wins since 2024; now rated 73 in that sphere; 80-1 for Naas hurdling/stable debut when 29l behind; others more likely.
runners.comment: Three-time Flat winner but no wins since 2024; beaten 29l on hurdling/stable debut

runners.name: Leo Messy
runners.spotlight: Always in rear when tailed off at Naas on debut (2m3f, soft to heavy); others preferred.
runners.comment: Always in rear when tailed off at Naas on debut; others preferred

runners.name: True Harmony
runners.spotlight: Won a point-to-point but it's been a struggle in three maiden hurdles; may do better in handicaps.
runners.comment: Point winner has struggled in maiden hurdles; may do better in h'caps

runners.name: Westin Pimpernel
runners.spotlight: Sent off at 250-1 on debut at Naas and ran like it; unlikely.
runners.comment: Sent off at 250-1 on debut at Naas and ran like it; unlikely

runners.name: Seabloom
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; finished 15th of 18 in the valuable Punchestown bumper 334 days ago; tongue-tied now and improvement very possible; however, Townend prefers Robertjames.
runners.comment: 15th of 18 in valuable Punchestown bumper 334 days ago; Townend rides Robertjames

races.name: Hood McGowan Kirk Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Toor Moon
runners.spotlight: Consistent form last autumn, finishing placed five times back-to-back, including at this track (3m, yielding); 2026 form is below that level; tailed off over fences at Tramore in cheekpieces; decent fourth at Thurles (2m4f, heavy); never travelled when tailed off at Limerick last time (2m5f, heavy); needs to come back to his best.
runners.comment: Consistent form last Autumn but 2026 runs below that; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Saunton Surf
runners.spotlight: Some decent form when trained in Britain; best of three runs by far for new yard came last time at Down Royal when a 7.75l fifth (behind Coppola); cheekpieces on now.
runners.comment: Some decent British form and best Irish run came last time when fifth

runners.name: Coppola
runners.spotlight: Maiden point-to-pointer ran her best race by far when a runner-up on stable debut at Down Royal last time (2m5f, yielding), rallied and kept on that day; up 3lb but she's a player.
runners.comment: Maiden pointer ran best race by far when runner-up on stable debut last time; player

runners.name: Mister Persistence
runners.spotlight: Long odds for all 12 starts and yet to display any winning potential; failed to complete four of last five starts; new headgear combination tried.
runners.comment: Long odds for all 12 starts and yet to display any winning potential

runners.name: Lets Go Mans
runners.spotlight: Up 7lb following neck win at Naas last time on handicap debut in a first-time tongue tie (retained); needed every yard to get up that day but those involved at the finish have been placed subsequently; cheekpieces go on now; may improve again and big chance of following up.
runners.comment: Up 7lb following neck win on h'cap debut; may improve again and big chance of following up

runners.name: Marine Ranger
runners.spotlight: Kept on for third at Fairyhouse in December (2m4f, soft), although 16l behind the winner; tailed off there next time on New Year's Day (3m2f, yielding); good effort at Naas last time when a 5.25l fourth behind the winner Let's Go Mans (3m, soft to heavy); can get involved.
runners.comment: 5.25l fourth behind Let's Go Mans last time; can get involved

runners.name: Jimlis Cave
runners.spotlight: Sole win came here in a 2m6f handicap hurdle off this mark in 2024 (good to yielding); reverts to hurdles following poor efforts in his last two runs; won't want the ground too soft.
runners.comment: Sole win came in a 2m6f h'cap off this mark in 2024; won't want ground too soft

runners.name: Robindevidastar
runners.spotlight: Veteran is 3-38 over hurdles, the last win coming at Punchestown (2m4f, good) in November 2024 off 84; decent fourth at Clonmel (2m4f, heavy) before dismal effort at Punchestown next time (2m3f, heavy); bounced back with rallying runner-up finish at Clonmel last time (2m, heavy); on career low mark and can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Veteran is 3-38 over hurdles; rallied for second last time; on career low mark

runners.name: The Son Of Brega
runners.spotlight: No show in maidens and has failed to complete in two of three starts in handicaps; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: No show in m'dens and failed to complete two of three starts in h'caps; hard to fancy

runners.name: Deanery Charlie
runners.spotlight: Has finished fourth in two handicap runs; stayed on well at Fairyhouse (2m4f, soft); hung left at Punchestown last time (2m3f, heavy); Let's Go Mans looks the pick of the stable's two.
runners.comment: Fourth in two h'cap runs; Let's Go Mans looks pick of stable's two

runners.name: Young And Foolish
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; tailed off in all three maiden hurdle starts at 250-1; pulled up on handicap debut at Naas at 80-1 (3m, soft to heavy).
runners.comment: Reserve; tailed off in m'dens; PU at 80-1 on h'cap debut

runners.name: Bruno From Mars
runners.spotlight: First reserve; produced a couple of encouraging runs at around 2m in December and January but never involved upped to 2m4f at Punchestown latest; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Reserve; some encouraging runs at 2m in Dec'/Jan'; never involved upped to 2m4f latest

runners.name: Shanroe Nancy
runners.spotlight: Hasn't improved for handicaps; pulled up at Down Royal last time; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: Hasn't improved for handicaps; pulled up at Down Royal last time; hard to make a case for

runners.name: Cant Catch Molly
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; finished in mid-division in two of her three starts in maidens; dropped 2lb following a 17.5l tenth of 17 on handicap debut at Naas (2m, soft to heavy).
runners.comment: Reserve; down 2lb following 17.5l tenth of 17 on h'cap debut

runners.name: Kalsman
runners.spotlight: 1-12 over hurdles, the win coming over C&D off 88 in June 2024 (good); just one run since then, in this race last year when fourth (good); may need this, and ground won't suit.
runners.comment: C&D winner in 2024; just one run since then, in this race last year; may need this

runners.name: Ocean Murphy
runners.spotlight: Made late headway in his last two runs in an academy hurdle at Cork and a maiden hurdle at Gowran at around 2m when well beaten; interestingly upped to this trip for handicap debut; improvement feasible and market check advised.
runners.comment: Late headway in last two runs; interestingly upped to this trip for h'cap debut

runners.name: Cozone
runners.spotlight: Two-time hurdles winner lost way since a good run at Punchestown in November 2024; well handicapped on best form but lacks a recent run; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Dual hurdles winner lost way in 2025; lacks a recent run and others appeal more

runners.name: Appian Way
runners.spotlight: Dual winning hurdler but no wins since successful here in 2022; poor form in 2025; warmed up for this with run at Dundalk in February, finishing 28l behind,
runners.comment: No wins since 2022; poor 2025 form; 28l behind at Dundalk last time

races.name: Billie And Bertie FitzGerald Memorial Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Blue Reed
runners.spotlight: Now a 20 race maiden over hurdles; his run at Punchestown on New Year's Eve when a runner-up would give him a definite chance; however, he has been beaten by 26l+ in his last two runs.
runners.comment: 0-20 over hurdles; runner-up on New Year's Eve but beaten 26l+ last twice

runners.name: Gamigin
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner returned to form when second of 11 finishers at Down Royal (2m1f, soft) in January off 3lb lower; weak in the market for Ayr run last time, weakening quickly to finish last of six finishers; in the mix at his best.
runners.comment: Three-time hurdles winner returned to form when second in Jan'; Ayr run last time was poor

runners.name: Dairy Force
runners.spotlight: Good ground Kilbeggan maiden hurdle winner for Liam O'Brien; sent off the 15-8 joint-favourite at Fairyhouse on stable debut but weakened into a 21l fourth on heavy ground; drying ground would be in his favour.
runners.comment: Good ground m'den hurdle winner was 21l behind on stable debut; drying ground will help

runners.name: Splashing Out
runners.spotlight: A win apiece over hurdles and fences; reverts to hurdles now following three poor efforts since his chase win at Down Royal in October; others preferred.
runners.comment: Hurdles/chase winner; reverts to timber following three poor runs since  chase win in Oct'

runners.name: Dunany Point
runners.spotlight: 1-13 over hurdles, the win coming at Bellewstown (2m5f, good) last year; heavy defeat at Naas latest but may have needed that; should come on for that but may be on a tough mark (11lb above win) and wants good ground.
runners.comment: 1-13 over hurdles; may come on for run last time but possibly on a tough mark

runners.name: Brave Soldier
runners.spotlight: Maiden has posted some good runs in defeat up to 2m5f on goodish ground but opposable on balance of form
runners.comment: M'den has posted some decent runs in defeat on goodish ground but opposable on balance

runners.name: Metamorpheus
runners.spotlight: No further success since winning a Punchestown maiden hurdle (2m, heavy) three years ago; ran well from time to time when trained by Richard O'Brien; hard to fancy on evidence of three starts for this stable.
runners.comment: No further success since m'den hurdle three years ago; hard to fancy on recent starts

runners.name: Coolboy Christy
