runners.spotlight: Best run of his life came in Leopardstown handicap debut in novice company earlier this month (2m3f, soft to heavy), keeping on well for a 4.5l fourth; that run can be marked up as he fared best of those coming from off the pace; up 2lb but player.
runners.comment: Best career run came on h'cap debut last time when fourth; player

runners.name: Lips Freedom
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner in Germany, not nearly as effective a hurdler and never counted in two starts in handicap company; opposable in first-time tongue tie.
runners.comment: Three-time Flat winner in Germany; not as effective as a hurdler; opposable

runners.name: Your Call
runners.spotlight: AW nursery winner in December; some minor promise in her opening two hurdle runs for this yard, but tailed off at Gowran last time; makes handicap debut now.
runners.comment: AW nursery winner in Dec'; minor promise in opening hurdle runs; makes h'cap debut

races.name: Kevin Ross Bloodstock 110 Series Final Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Themanintheboots
runners.spotlight: Bagged third career hurdles win at Navan in November (2m4f, soft to heavy) off 98; poor in four starts since; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Bagged third career hurdles win at Navan in Nov'; poor in four starts since

runners.name: Miss Lia
runners.spotlight: Tramore maiden hurdle winner last season (2m5f, good to yielding); some good runs in defeat this term, with runner-up finishes three times up to 2m7f on different ground types; always behind when pulled up at Fairyhouse latest; capable of getting involved at her best.
runners.comment: M'den hurdle winner was a runner-up three times this term; always behind when PU latest
runners.owner: Sussex By The Sea Syndicate/David LEstrange

runners.name: Couldntmakeitup
runners.spotlight: 0-14 under rules'; some placed efforts, including when a 4l third at Punchestown when rallying well (2m, soft to heavy); needs to bounce back from dismal run at Newbury last time (2m5f, good to soft).
runners.comment: 0-14 under rules; 4l third at Punchestown but dismal run at Newbury latest

runners.name: Lough Nigara
runners.spotlight: Quirky sort who can sometimes be reluctant to race; got a deserved first win after a series of fine runs in defeat at Punchestown in January (2m6f, soft to heavy); beaten just a neck back there next time; bagged another win at Thurles earlier this month, rallying to get back up by a nose despite rider dropping his whip; up 7lb but in great form.
runners.comment: Reluctant to race sometimes; up 7lb for his neck win last time but in wonderful form

runners.name: Bergamasquo
runners.spotlight: Dropped 2lb after being beaten 29l behind Lough Nigara at Thurles on handicap debut last time (2m7f, soft to heavy); tongue tie and cheekpieces go on; Donoghue jumps ship to Take Stock.
runners.comment: Beaten 29l behind Lough Nigara on h'cap debut last time; Donoghue rides Take Stock

runners.name: Midleton Rare
runners.spotlight: Blows hot and cold; got first hurdling victory at Limerick in October (2m4f, soft); decent third sandwiched between two poor efforts following his win; winner of big-field Fairyhouse handicap on New Year's Day (2m4f, yielding) off 4lb lower under Kennedy; not at his best in last four outings; maybe the return of Kennedy can spark him.
runners.comment: Poor since his second hurdles win in Jan'; maybe return of Kennedy can spark him

runners.name: John Storey
runners.spotlight: Big-field Fairyhouse handicap hurdle winner on New Year's Day 2025 off 1lb higher (3m1f, yielding to soft); well beaten in final starts for Liam Cusack; positives to take from his 10.5l fourth at Punchestown on stable debut in this headgear; in the mix if building on that.
runners.comment: Positives to take from fourth on stable debut last time; 1lb below winning mark

runners.name: Better Times
runners.spotlight: No real impression when a 12.5l seventh on handicap debut (2m3f, soft to heavy) at Leopardstown; more needed to get involved
runners.comment: No real impression on h'cap debut last time; more needed to get involved

runners.name: Take Stock
runners.spotlight: Hint of promise in maidens and solid effort on Naas handicap debut over 2m3f when a distant third (2m3f, yielding); in fourth but no real impression when a faller at the last at Punchestown (2m3f, soft to heavy), in a race where Lough Nigara was a neck second; Donoghue back aboard now.
runners.comment: Solid h'cap debut before fell at last when fourth latest (Lough Nigara in second that day)

runners.name: Kanog Bay
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in his second handicap start at Fairyhouse in January (2m4f, soft); hit with a 10lb rise for that and pulled up when upped to 2m7f at Thurles (signs of delayed recovery post race); needs to bounce back with a tongue tie added but big contender if he does.
runners.comment: Off the mark in second h'cap start off 10lb lower; needs to bounce back after PU last time

runners.name: Gangster Granny
runners.spotlight: Dual purpose; two-time Flat winner for Jessica Harrington; heavy defeats at hefty odds in four starts for this yard (one on AW); hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Heavy defeats in four starts for this yard at hefty odds; hard to fancy

races.name: Randox Ulster National Handicap Chase

runners.name: Rexem
runners.spotlight: Mixed form in 2025; ran his best race for a long time when going close at Musselburgh on New Year's Day (2m4f, good to soft); again finished second over 2m7f at that venue last time; main positive is his good record at this venue, two wins (bumper and maiden hurdle) and a second from four visits.
runners.comment: Twice second at Musselburgh in recent months, has a good record at this venue

runners.name: Brideswell Lad
runners.spotlight: Six-time chase winner; did not make it to the track in 2025; pulled up in a point-to-point on comeback; well beaten in two runs over hurdles in recent months; riders' claim is the main positive,
runners.comment: Six-time chase winner, no sign of his best form since returning fron a long absence

runners.name: Slanagaibhgoleir
runners.spotlight: 122 rated hurdler; got off the mark at the second attempt over fences in a 2m4f beginners' event at Down Royal at Christmas; beaten 6 1/4l in a handicap chase at Gowran, where heavy ground may not have been ideal; better when second in a rated race at Down Royal; hard to be sure how effectively he will stay.
runners.comment: Useful form over 2m4f at Down Royal, hard to be sure how effectively he will stay

runners.name: Solly Attwell
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back chase wins at Galway (2m7f, good to yielding) and this venue (3m, yielding) last September; best of five subsequent runs when afair sixth at Punchestown (2m7f, soft to heavy) in January; struggled over hurdles last time; first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Gained back-to-back wins last autumn, struggled over hurdles last time,   blinkered now

runners.name: Beggars Rock
runners.spotlight: Won a Kilbeggan beginners' chase (2m4f, good) last May for Declan Queally; placed over 2m4f in the first of two outings for Gordon Elliott in January; has joined last year's winning trainer who has three others guaranteed a run; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, has joined last year's winning trainer who has three guaranteed runners

runners.name: Bodhisattva
runners.spotlight: Third of five finishers in the Edinburgh National at Musselburgh in February; pulled up at Bangor and tailed off last ast of five finishers  in the 4m2f Midlands National at Uttoxter; finished third in this event in 2024; last success was in 2023; second reserve.
runners.comment: Second reserve, third in this event in 2024, unconvincing recent form, last win in 2023

runners.name: Uncle Pat
runners.spotlight: Bumper/hurdles winner; has run well in defeat over fences, including when second over 2m3f in the Tim Duggan at Limerick; unseated at the DRF meeting at Leopardstown; strong chance now shown reserves of stamina when keeping on for second in the 3m3f Grand National Trial at Punchestown.
runners.comment: Second in Punchestown's Grand National Trial, does not need to improve much to win here
runners.owner: Patrick Corbett & Michael Corbett & J J Corbett & W Clancy

runners.name: Dorans Law
runners.spotlight: Stays well and handled yielding-to-soft when winning a 3m2f handicap hurdle at Ballinrobe two seasons ago; however, seems best on a sound surface; won on good ground at Wexford last summer; absent since a 12l second at the Listowel festival.
runners.comment: Won on good ground at Wexford last summer, off since 12l second at the Listowel festival

runners.name: Brandt
runners.spotlight: Good form last winter, notably winning a 3m4f handicap chase at Leopardstown; struggled in the spring in the Scottish National and in a valuable Punchestown handicap; should be better for a recent hurdles run.
runners.comment: Won a 3m4f handicap chase at Leopardstown last season, should be better for a hurdles run

runners.name: St Deniss Well
runners.spotlight: Mixed form this season, best efforts over hurdles at Navan, winning 2m handicap and second to a progressive horse over 2m4f last time; two less encouraging runs over fences, well beaten in sixth in the 3m6f Porterstown Chase and pulled up at Leopardstown when found to have blood at both nostrils; trainer won this last year.
runners.comment: Best form this term over hurdles; could go well for the trainer who won this last year

runners.name: I A Connect
runners.spotlight: Veteran gained his second win over fences and fifth all in a handicap over an extended 2m4f at Down Royal's Christmas meeting; 12lb higher now and has finished down the field over hurdles on his last two runs.
runners.comment: Now 12lb higher than for Down Royal win, down the field over hurdles on his last two runs

runners.name: Born Braver
runners.spotlight: 2-4 over hurdles; modest form in beginners' chases; much improved when a neck second over 3m at Down Royal on second handicap start, underlining the stamina shown when winning two points and a 3m hurdle; however, he failed to build on that in the Grand National Trial at Punchestown.
runners.comment: Second at Down Royal on second handicap start, well held by Uncle Pat on  Punchestown run

runners.name: Money Heist
runners.spotlight: Won three times for Gavin Cromwell; signed off last season with second in 20-runner handicap hurdle at Fairyhouse (2m4f, soft to heavy); two runs for this stable have been over hurdles; first time with a tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Two runs for this stable have been over hurdles, first time with a tongue-tie

runners.name: Flash De Touzaine
runners.spotlight: Gained the most recent of his three chase wins in a 2m3f handicap on good ground at Ballinrone last May; lacks a recent run and is getting on in years, but stays well and is reasonably ground-versatile
runners.comment: Lacks a recent run and is getting on in years, but stays well and seems ground-versatile
runners.owner: J Carthy & Eamon Nolan & Kieran P Ryan & Mrs Avril Doyle

runners.name: Fishery Lane
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle over 2m4f; 0-5 chasing this term, modest placings behind stablemates in two beginners' events; fair effort when  fourth at Leopardstown in his first handicap chase; open to improvement, but stablemate Uncle Pat appears more attractively handicapped
runners.comment: Fair fourth on handicap debut, stablemate Uncle Pat appears more attractively handicapped

runners.name: Ontheropes
runners.spotlight: Point-to-winner last season before going hunter chasing; no success on the track since 2021; has shown fair form over hurdles this season; 1-23 record over fences is a negative.
runners.comment: Has shown fair form over hurdles this season, 1-23 record over fences is a negative

runners.name: The Gradual Slope
runners.spotlight: Won a valuable staying handicap chase at the 2024 Punchestown festival; ordinary form since then, although did well to maintain contact with top-class performers when 66-1 outsider of four at Down Royal on St Patrick's Day; flattered by proximity but the run implies a return to form; third reserve.
runners.comment: Third reserve, not disgraced when taking on very smart types at Down Royal

runners.name: Shannon Royale
runners.spotlight: Won a 3m beginners' chase for Gordon Elliott last season; lost form and has failed to show anything like his best since joining this team; reported to have bled from the nose at Cheltenham handicap on New Year's Day; reportedly bled again in Punchestown cross-country event
runners.comment: Has failed to show anything like his best since joining this team, has reportedly bled

races.name: Caherty Stables Beginners Chase

runners.name: Karafon
runners.spotlight: Best of his form chase runs when second at Ayr in January, two moderate runs since then; possibly flattered by an official rating of 120.
runners.comment: Best of his form chase runs when second at Ayr in January, two moderate runs since then

runners.name: Strutter
runners.spotlight: Winner of his last two handicap hurdle races seven months apart, at Uttoxeter for Nicky Henderson in May 2024 and on first run for Willie Mullins at Limerick in December that year; ought to be capable of much better than at Leopardstown on chasing; far too keen then, and dropped away very quickly before the last; tongue-tie now in addition to hood.
runners.comment: Ought to be capable of much better than at Leopardstown, far too keen then, tongue-tie now

runners.name: Find A Fortune
runners.spotlight: Consistent if frustrating in maiden hurdles before winning a 2m4f rated event at Fairyhouse early last year (soft); a latecomer in chasing; faded late in the Leopardstown event in which the much shorter-priced Strutter flopped.
runners.comment: Took a long time to win a race over hurdles, faded late at Leopardstown in his first chase

runners.name: Dees Lady
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in both maiden hurdles last season; looked in need of the run on return in decent company at Naas in December; bounced back from a poor Naas run when making a winning handicap debut at Leopardstown; has the scope to make a chaser; noteworthy she is seen straight over fences now after that success.
runners.comment: Fair maiden hurdle form last season, came good in a handicap on third run this term

runners.name: Littlefoot
runners.spotlight: Promising bumper horse early in career with form linked to Nick Rockett and Dancing City; 1-14 over hurdles; no chasing experience; up against it.
runners.comment: Promising bumper horse early in career, 1-14 over hurdles, no chasing experience

runners.name: Billy Lee Swagger
runners.spotlight: Ran out an easy winner of a Fairyhouse handicap in November (2m5f, yielding to soft); had a tack malfunction when favourite to follow up at the same venue; less easy to ignore a dismal Naas run.
runners.comment: Stylish winner of a handicap hurdle in November, tack issues next time, no excuse latest

runners.name: Mercury Mission
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back winner of novice handicap hurdles in March/April last year for Philip Rothwell; won again in the autumn; makes stable debut after a break; would prefer better ground
runners.comment: Enjoyed a good spell over hurdles around this time last year, makes stable debut

runners.name: Icare dAubrelle
runners.spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner; has had training issues over the years; nothing in his current form gives rise to optimism; pulled up in two of his last three races.
runners.comment: Has had training issues over the years, nothing in his current form gives rise to optimism

runners.name: Great Universe
runners.spotlight: Won a Clonmel bumper in 2023, and took a 3m2f Sligo maiden hurdle last season; not a betting proposition after a fall and a refusal on his first two runs over fences.
runners.comment: Not a betting proposition after a fall and a refusal on his first two runs over fences

races.name: Boardsmill Sires Maiden Hunters Chase

runners.name: Magic Sadler
runners.spotlight: Winner of nine of his 18 starts in points; has recorded five wins and three seconds since October for the very capable Emily Costello; however, he put a blot on his record when unseating his rider at the first in a hunter chase at Thurles early last month.
runners.comment: Nine wins in points, unseated at Thurles in January should go close with  a clear round

runners.name: Cause For Comment
runners.spotlight: Twice runner-up in hunter chases last season; beaten favourite both times; solid form behind his useful stablemate Asphalt Cowboy in this race 12 months ago before a less inspiring effort at Tipperary; beat a subsequent winner handily in a maiden point at Punchestown early last month; this looks like a suitable opportunity.
runners.comment: A wide-margin point winner, more mature now than when second here 12 month ago

runners.name: Lawful Ruler
runners.spotlight: Has won only once from ten starts in points; produced a career-best in defeat against West Of Idaho at Tyrella just over three weeks ago.
runners.comment: 1-10 in point,  career-best in defeat against West Of Idaho at Tyrella this month

runners.name: West Of Idaho
runners.spotlight: Let down by his jumping during the autumn points campaign; has got the hang of things to win his two most recent races at Tyrella, beating Lawful Ruler on the second occasion.
runners.comment: Jumping problems seem to have been resolved, beat Lawful Ruler at Tyrella last time

runners.name: Yewtree Hill
runners.spotlight: Third of five finishers in a similar race to this her last May; modest form in handicap chases last summer; has won both starts since returning to points (overall record, 4-11); in between, not disgraced when around 20l fourth behind the smart Cons Roc in a hunter chase at Fairyhouse.
runners.comment: Has won both starts since returning to points this season, not disgraced in a hunter chase

runners.name: Mister Frostie
runners.spotlight: Hints of ability in maiden hurdles for this stable; won a maiden point at Hexham last November; hard to fancy on the form of two runs since returning to Colin McBratney.
runners.comment: Point winner in Britain, hard to fancy on the form of two runs since returning to Ireland

runners.name: Sledgehammer
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of a maiden point at Tyrella early in the month after two failures to complete; tailed off on hunter chase debut at Down Royal 12 days ago.
runners.comment: Narrow winner of a point, tailed off on hunter chase debut at Down Royal 12 days ago

runners.name: Cebu City
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on his only hunter chase start last season; runner-up in three points for Sonny Carey this term; two respectable displays before failing to land the odds in a four-runner race last time; unlikely to cope with stablemate Cause For Comment.
runners.comment: Runner-up in three points this term, unlikely to cope with stablemate Cause For Comment

runners.name: Tumuch
runners.spotlight: Fetched 335,000GBP after winning a maiden at Lingstown in 2023; placed once over hurdles for Gordon Elliott; showed little or no promise over jumps last season; won the first of two starts in points for his new owner Jamie Dwyer and now has first run for Tom Dreaper, who saddled two winners in points last weekend.
runners.comment: Promising horse early in career, failed to prosper for Gordon Elliott, now a point winner

races.name: Chung Chi Alumni Handicap (Class 5) (Course A+3) (Turf)

runners.name: Smiling Emperor
runners.spotlight: Decent run two starts back but returned to modest mode last time and he's unconvincing.
runners.comment: Decent run two starts back but returned to modest mode last time and he's unconvincing

runners.name: My Flying Angel
runners.spotlight: Third in a trial this month; only local win came over C&D and he arrives in good form, finishing close up behind Winning Machine over C&D and holding his own back at 6f a month ago; not ideally drawn but has a big effort in him.
runners.comment: Only local win came over C&D and he arrives in good form; not ideally drawn, however
runners.owner: Annie Cheung King Wai & Gavin Cheung King Chi

runners.name: Shine Bright
runners.spotlight: Uncompetitive this season (6f-1m) and struggles continued in a recent barrier trial.
runners.comment: Uncompetitive this season (6f-1m) and struggles continued in a recent barrier trial

runners.name: Winning Machine
runners.spotlight: Excellent run from a wide stall here last month (7f) and that was his stable debut; has since trailed home last in a barrier trial, but that latest official effort makes him a player.
runners.comment: Ran well over C&D from a wide stall on last month's stable debut; a player
runners.owner: Sammy Ma Hon Kit/Chan Sin Shui/Exec Of Est Of J C T Cheung

runners.name: Bling Bling Genius
runners.spotlight: Has yet to win but has threatened and was a close fourth over C&D three weeks ago; there were no excuses that day from a handy position but he needs considering.
runners.comment: Has yet to win but has threatened and was a close fourth over C&D three weeks ago

runners.name: Rich Horse
runners.spotlight: Won in Ireland but his Hong Kong losing run is mounting up and he's poorly drawn.
runners.comment: Won in Ireland but his Hong Kong losing run is mounting up and he's poorly drawn

runners.name: Ace
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but beaten only 2l over 6f here last time despite a wide draw and misfortune in running; stays 1m so 7f won't be a problem and he's also been contesting Class 4s; interesting on first run for Mark Newnham; fourth in a recent trial.
runners.comment: 0-8 but beaten 2l over 6f here last time despite a wide draw and misfortune in running

runners.name: Power Summit
runners.spotlight: Maiden who failed to fire when dropped into a Class 5 last time and again in a recent barrier trial; also ran poorly the last time he tried 7f.
runners.comment: Failed to fire when dropped into a Class 5 last time and again in a recent barrier trial

runners.name: He Was Me
runners.spotlight: The win was a long time coming but he did well to defy a wide draw over C&D three weeks ago when Bling Bling Genius was back in fourth; Andrea Atzeni rides again off a 5lb higher mark; considered.
runners.comment: The win was a long time coming but did well to defy a wide draw over C&D three weeks ago

runners.name: Gangnam Star
runners.spotlight: Close up over 1m here in November but well beaten since (that trip and 6f); an infrequent winner at the best of times and recent trial form nothing to shout about.
runners.comment: An infrequent winner at the best of times and recent trial form nothing to shout about
runners.owner: Francis & Louise Chan And Aaron Chan

runners.name: Manythanks Forever
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner who didn't have things go all his way when behind Winning Machine and The Flying Angel here last month; however, his inconsistency is forever a worry and he was beaten in a recent trial.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner but his inconsistency is forever a worry
runners.owner: Chan Wing Kwong Patrick Ng Pak Hong Et Al

runners.name: Happy Buddies
runners.spotlight: Another runner who is downgraded from Class 4s but this maiden needs a career best from stall 13; that seems unlikely, especially after trailing home last in a recent barrier trial.
runners.comment: Downgraded but this maiden needs a career best from stall 13; that seems unlikely

runners.name: Mazing Grace
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who doesn't appear to be progressing and best trip remains questionable.
runners.comment: Eight-race maiden who doesn't appear to be progressing and best trip remains questionable

runners.name: One Love
runners.spotlight: Reserve; very opposable on his form in Hong Kong; only fourth in a trial since last official run.
runners.comment: Reserve; opposable on form in Hong Kong; only fourth in a trial since last official run

runners.name: Double Show
runners.spotlight: Won comfortably over C&D in a refitted visor in October and followed that with a second on his return visit; can be excused latest defeat at Happy Valley as probably didn't stay the mile; only seventh in a trial since.
runners.comment: Probably failed to stay the 1m last time and his 7f form here is much respected

runners.name: Telecom Power
runners.spotlight: Reserve; hard to win with but bang there in his last three races over 1m at Happy Valley and that trip seems to stretch him; could go well if getting in.
runners.comment: Reserve; hard to win with but bang there in his last three races over 1m at Happy Valley
runners.owner: Cheung King Shek Billy Cheung King Shan Et Al

races.name: New Asia Alumni Handicap (Class 4) (Course A+3) (Turf)

runners.name: So My Folks
runners.spotlight: Finished a 2l fourth over 6f here two weeks ago but had no excuses from a good position and he has stamina to prove upped to 7f.
runners.comment: Finished a 2l fourth over 6f here two weeks ago but had no excuses from a good position

runners.name: Shotgun
runners.spotlight: Made all for Class 5 honours last month and has held his own twice since at this loftier level, at 6f and 7f; tactically versatile and won a recent trial; lots to like.
runners.comment: Made all in a Class 5 last month and has held his own twice since at this loftier level

runners.name: The Lion King
runners.spotlight: A winner in Australia and his first placing in Hong Kong when third last time; however, he couldn't cope with the front two and dropping back to 7f is a worry.
runners.comment: first local placing last time but this drop back to 7f is a worry

runners.name: Apolar Fighter
runners.spotlight: Class 5 wins late last year at Happy Valley over 1m; his recent runs here have been over 7f but he could still have run better.
runners.comment: Dual 1m winner at Happy Valley; down the field the last twice here over 7f

runners.name: The All Rounder
runners.spotlight: Runner-up over C&D in the first-time eyeshield and ran pretty well to be sixth last time from where he was turning in; however, as was the case that day, the draw hasn't been kind.
runners.comment: Two good runs since the eyeshield went on but again the draw hasn't been kind

runners.name: Invictus Dragon
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but he's not always enjoyed the rub of the green, as when a closing third here four weeks ago; has the same stall 12 to contend with but he's unexposed over this longer trip; Zac Purton keeps the faith.
runners.comment: Again drawn wide but still ran well over 6f here last time; unexposed at 7f

runners.name: Russet Glow
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced in any of his three runs over 6f and gives the impression that 7f will suit; open to improvement.
runners.comment: Not disgraced in any of his three runs over 6f and gives the impression that 7f will suit

runners.name: Danicas Choice
runners.spotlight: Won in New Zealand; still early days in Hong Kong and he appreciated the drop into a Class 4 when only 2l away over 1m here three weeks ago; however, he still finished only tenth and he was keeping on, so dropping back to 7f might not be ideal.
runners.comment: Keeping on over 1m here last time so dropping back to 7f might not be ideal

runners.name: Honorary
runners.spotlight: No evidence that he's well handicapped and he was only fourth in his latest trial.
runners.comment: No evidence that he's well handicapped and he was only fourth in his latest trial
runners.owner: Yip Yuk Kit & Dr Tommy Yip Chun Ming

runners.name: Strathpeffer
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner and was running well until failing to overcome a wide draw here in early February; he's dropped on the same box and was only fourth in a recent trial.
runners.comment: In form until poorly drawn last time and he's dropped on the same box

runners.name: Lets Have Fun
runners.spotlight: Ability in Australia but Hong Kong form is moderate and the step up to 7f and drop into a Class 4 failed to improve matters last time.
runners.comment: The step up to 7f and drop into a Class 4 failed to improve matters last time

runners.name: Always Ahead
runners.spotlight: Won in Australia; hasn't been looking a winner waiting to happen in Hong Kong, even last time when relegated into a Class 4; raised to 7f here but probably needs further help from the handicapper.
runners.comment: Won in Australia; hasn't been looking a winner waiting to happen in Hong Kong
runners.owner: Wong Chung Yin & Dr Luk Che Chung

runners.name: Glorious St Pauls
runners.spotlight: Won a trial this month but has since finished only fifth in another; couldn't recommend on what he's shown in two races here over C&D.
runners.comment: Couldn't recommend on what he's shown in two races here over C&D

runners.name: View Of Cosmos
runners.spotlight: Finished last in his trial and suffered the same fate on official debut over 6f at Happy Valley (46-1).
runners.comment: Finished last in a trial and suffered same fate on official debut over 6f at Happy Valley

runners.name: Graceful Heart
runners.spotlight: Reserve; makes official debut after finishing 9212 in his trials; clearly has plenty of talent.
runners.comment: Reserve; makes official debut after finishing 9212 in his trials; clearly has talent

races.name: United Alumni Handicap (Class 4) (Course A+3) (Turf)

runners.name: Fortune Kingo
runners.spotlight: No wins in seven and was behind a few of these here four weeks ago; hard to warm to.
runners.comment: No wins in seven and was behind a few of these here four weeks ago; hard to warm to

runners.name: Dilbagh
runners.spotlight: Promising fourth at Happy Valley in September (1m1f) but hasn't remotely threatened since; very opposable.
runners.comment: Fourth at Happy Valley in September (1m1f) but hasn't remotely threatened since

runners.name: Agenda
runners.spotlight: Recent run in a trial wasn't that encouraging but this 5yo hasn't been running at all badly in Class 3 company and there are positives here, with Zac Purton taking over off a reduced mark and with a good draw to boot.
runners.comment: Zac Purton takes over off a reduced mark and with a good draw to boot
runners.owner: Albert Hung Chao Hong & Stephanie Hung Yujie

runners.name: Volcanic Spark
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner, at 7f and 1m1f; definite chance on his January form here but only eighth last time in a race dominated by Storm Runner and Charity Together; has since disappointed in a barrier trial.
runners.comment: Only eighth last time in a race dominated by Storm Runner and Charity Together

runners.name: Charity Together
runners.spotlight: Three wins in Britain; has yet to deliver in Hong Kong but he's getting there and pushed Storm Runner close over 1m1f here four weeks ago; has a 6lb pull on the winner so clearly has claims; has finished last in a trial since.
runners.comment: Pushed Storm Runner close over 1m1f here and has a 6lb pull with that rival

runners.name: Star Brose
runners.spotlight: 1m3f winner at Happy Valley; no match for the winner over that same C&D last time but it was a notable run from well off the pace and Double Win was in third; respected.
runners.comment: Had Double Win behind when runner-up at Happy Valley last time; in the mix

runners.name: Splendid Force
runners.spotlight: 0-5; needs the change of stable and removal of headgear to make a difference; third in a recent trial.
runners.comment: 0-5; needs the change of stable and removal of headgear to make a difference
runners.owner: Colin Chu Wan Kin & Mandy Luo Wen

runners.name: Supreme Mastermind
runners.spotlight: Only ninth when back in a Class 4 four weeks ago (Storm Runner won that race) and it's a negative that Zac Purton was aboard for a recent trial win but not now.
runners.comment: Maiden; it's a negative that Zac Purton was aboard for a recent trial win but not now

runners.name: Grey Charger
runners.spotlight: Has regressed since arriving from Britain and could have run better on his relegation into a Class 4 last time.
runners.comment: Looks regressive and could have run better on his relegation into a Class 4 last time

runners.name: Storm Runner
runners.spotlight: Took a while to get his head in front but has now gone back-to-back over 1m1f, at Happy Valley then here when defying a 5lb rise (both Class 4s); now has another 7lb to deal with but he was good value for his last win and something has clicked.
runners.comment: Up 7lb but good value for his last win and something has clicked

runners.name: Serangoon
runners.spotlight: Exploited a reduced mark over 1m1f here in February but was never that involved at Happy Valley last time and he has form there.
runners.comment: Course winner in Feb; never that involved at Happy Valley last time and he has form there

runners.name: Double Win
runners.spotlight: Beat Supreme Mastermind here in November and followed up at Happy Valley (1m3f); another fair run last time when close up behind Star Brose but current mark is looking an issue.
runners.comment: Hasn't looked ahead of his mark since back-to-back wins earlier this season
runners.owner: Dr Kelvin Chong Man Yuk & Ma Yuen Yu

races.name: Shaw Alumni Handicap (Class 2) (Course A+3) (Turf)

runners.name: Pocketing
runners.spotlight: 5-17 when based in Australia, the latest win gained in 1m1f handicap in December; not an easy one to weigh up on this Hong Kong debut but his latest local trial was promising and the booking of Zac Purton catches the eye.
runners.comment: 5-17 when based in Australia; booking of Zac Purton catches the eye on Hong Kong debut

runners.name: Sword Point
runners.spotlight: Smart in his prime but has regressed this season; soundly beaten on his last two starts (both 1m1f) and needs to find a way to stop the rot.
runners.comment: Smart in his prime but has regressed this season; needs to stop the rot

runners.name: Max Que
runners.spotlight: Progressive 5yo who has collected three wins (7f-1m) this season and lost out only narrowly when placed on his last two appearances; up in grade and deserted by Zac Purton today but still commands respect.
runners.comment: Progressive 5yo who has collected three wins (7f-1m) this season and is still in good form
runners.owner: Ricky Ng Yu Chiu & To Ngo Fung

runners.name: Sky Heart
runners.spotlight: Scored over 7f here on seasonal debut in October and has largely remained in good form since; drops back in trip after close third at Happy Valley (1m1f) last month; likely to be in the shake-up again.
runners.comment: Won over 7f here on seasonal debut in October and has largely remained in good form since

runners.name: Packing Angel
runners.spotlight: Excellent third in Hong Kong Derby (1m2f) a year ago but has shown his form on only one of three appearances this season, when fourth over C&D two starts ago; not an easy one to predict.
runners.comment: Has shown his form on only one of three appearances this season; not easy to predict

runners.name: Awesome Fluke
runners.spotlight: Two wins this season, the latter a career-best performance at Happy Valley (1m) last month; safely held off new mark there since, though, and too inconsistent to rely upon heavily.
runners.comment: Two wins this season; safely held off this mark last time, though, and not very consistent

runners.name: Joy Of Spring
runners.spotlight: Late-maturing 6yo who showed career-best form when driven-out winner over 1m1f in December; ran poorly over that trip here in January, though, and needs to rebound with another personal best.
runners.comment: Career-best form when winning in December; needs to rebound from a poor run in January
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Edward Hung & Doris Hung

runners.name: Californiatotality
runners.spotlight: Looked as good as ever when very close second at Happy Valley (1m1f) in October but returned from break with two underwhelming runs this year and remains on a fairly tough mark.
runners.comment: Back from break with two underwhelming runs this year; still on fairly tough mark

runners.name: Beauty Eternal
runners.spotlight: C&D Group 1 winner in his prime but very much on the downgrade now; moves back up to more suitable trip after midfield finish over 7f last month and has become too well handicapped to rule out but others have much more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: C&D Group 1 winner in his prime but very much on the downgrade now; others preferred

races.name: Faculty Of Education Alumni Handicap (Class 4) (Course A+3) (Turf)

runners.name: Quick Money
runners.spotlight: C&D novice winner last May but handicaps are proving a struggle; dropping in the weights but needs to show more.
runners.comment: C&D novice winner last May but handicaps are proving a struggle

runners.name: Ever Luck
runners.spotlight: Won from stall 14 on 6f debut in October; two good runs afterwards off this 8lb higher mark and latest defeat was in a Class 3; back at this level he's entitled to go well and looks sharp enough for this first crack at 5f; a high draw can be favourable over this C&D.
runners.comment: Lightly raced course winner over 6f; ran in a Class 3 latest; quick enough for 5f

runners.name: Comet Radiance
runners.spotlight: Improved form when going down by half a length to Run Run Sunrise here recently over 5f; had no excuses but clearly up to winning off this mark (raised 1lb);
runners.comment: Improved form when going down by half a length to Run Run Sunrise here recently over 5f

runners.name: Riding High
runners.spotlight: Reserve; his only win came over further at Happy Valley and that was a Class 5 (made all); has continued to run well, including here, but it's been a while since he ran over 5f.
runners.comment: Reserve; his only win came over further at Happy Valley and that was a Class 5

runners.name: Lucky Twenty
runners.spotlight: Shock winner on last summer's debut at Happy Valley (5f) and rediscovered some form when 2l off Run Run Sunrise here last time out; each-way claims on that form.
runners.comment: Rediscovered some form when 2l off Run Run Sunrise here last time out
runners.owner: 24/25 Jimmy K H Ting Trainer Syndicate

runners.name: Northern Fire Ball
runners.spotlight: Most of his racing and all three wins have been over 6f at Happy Valley; has form here but his two previous runs at 5f were underwhelming.
runners.comment: Most of his racing and all three wins have been over 6f at Happy Valley

runners.name: Beauty Thunder
runners.spotlight: Reserve; won over 7f in Ireland and added a 5f handicap at Happy Valley in October when 12lb lower; has shown he can deal with this mark with his recent seconds, though Happy Valley seems more his scene.
runners.comment: Reserve; dual winner and consistent, but Happy Valley seems more his scene

runners.name: Run Run Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 3yo who appears to be improving having won with a bit in hand over C&D three weeks ago with some of these behind; that triggered a 6lb rise but he could easily be capable of better again.
runners.comment: Raised 6lb for recent C&D success but could easily be capable of better again
runners.owner: Tang Ming James Tang Sau Yip Et Al

runners.name: Georgian Sigma
runners.spotlight: Had a wide trip when second to Ever Luck on 6f debut here in October and another fair effort the following month, for all that he was beaten at odds-on; subsequent form figures in trials are 534.
runners.comment: Two promising official runs and subsequent form figures in trials are 534
runners.owner: Clement Cheng Ka Man Alfred Choi & Roger Li Ka Chun

runners.name: Casa Primo
runners.spotlight: Built up some momentum in trials and from off the pace he wasn't far behind Run Run Sunrise on official debut three weeks ago (22-1); one to consider.
runners.comment: From off the pace he wasn't far behind Run Run Sunrise on official debut over C&D

runners.name: Perfect Triumph
runners.spotlight: Did win one of his barrier trials but his official debut here over 6f was forgettable.
runners.comment: Did win one of his barrier trials but his official debut here over 6f was forgettable

runners.name: Packing Phoenix
runners.spotlight: All four starts have been here over 6f; disappointing when odds-on last time and Zac Purton, who has been aboard for all his runs, deserts him for Turbo Jefferies; this 4yo has finished third in two recent trials.
runners.comment: All four starts were here over 6f; disappointing when odds-on last time for Zac Purton

runners.name: Synergy Express
runners.spotlight: Australian-bred 3yo whose form figures in trials this year read 56954; not the obvious answer.
runners.comment: Australian-bred 3yo whose form figures in trials this year read 56954

runners.name: Turbo Jefferies
runners.spotlight: Unable to win any of his three barrier trials this year but showed some ability and Zac Purton was aboard when third in the latest; needs considering.
runners.comment: Unable to win any of his three barrier trials this year but showed some ability
runners.owner: Simmy Leung Jan Lai & Benvinda Lai

runners.name: E Hopeful
runners.spotlight: Makes official debut on the back of a handful of defeats in trials and probably up against it.
runners.comment: Makes official debut on the back of a handful of defeats in trials; probably up against it

runners.name: Amo Ergo Sum
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in his last two trials but he finished last on debut here in November (6f); others safer.
runners.comment: Runner-up in his last two trials but he finished last on debut here in November (6f)

races.name: The Cuhk Alumni Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course A+3) (Turf)

runners.name: Peridot
runners.spotlight: Remains a maiden but not the first time he's threatened when a running-on third over C&D two weeks ago; from stall 4 he should be involved.
runners.comment: Not the first time he's threatened when a running-on third over C&D two weeks ago

runners.name: One More
runners.spotlight: Competitive form on the board and has been contesting Class 3s, but now 0-10 in Hong Kong and the draw is a negative.
runners.comment: Has some competitive form on the board and has been contesting Class 3s; bad draw
runners.owner: Dr & Mrs Johnny Ng Sai Chun Et Al

runners.name: Winning Now
runners.spotlight: Will need his best form yet on the track but it's still early days and he did win a recent trial under Karis Teetan.
runners.comment: Will need his best form yet on the track but did win a recent trial under Karis Teetan

runners.name: Francis Meynell
runners.spotlight: Useful juvenile in Ireland; yet to win in Hong Kong and there were no clear excuses when behind Circuit Fiery here this month; has since finished seventh in a trial.
runners.comment: There were no clear excuses when behind Circuit Fiery here last time
runners.owner: Albert Hung Chao Hong & Kevin Hung Dingteng

runners.name: Viva Firecracker
runners.spotlight: A never-dangerous fifth at Happy Valley latest but his run here the time before was a good one (at 6f) and he could be ready for another crack over this far.
runners.comment: His final run here was a good one (4th) and he looks worth another go over 7f

runners.name: King Dance
runners.spotlight: Overcame traffic issues to lead on the line over 6f here last month and, although well held off this 6lb higher mark three weeks ago, his late running suggested this longer trip might be a better fit; not drawn well, however.
runners.comment: 6f winner who could improve for going over 7f but he's not well drawn

runners.name: Flying Knight
runners.spotlight: Reserve; not without hope on his penultimate effort here as he was hot on the heels of the principals (7f); has disappointed since, however.
runners.comment: Reserve; not without hope on his penultimate effort; has disappointed since
runners.owner: Shi Ke Min & Frankie Fang Yuan

runners.name: Flying Christie
runners.spotlight: Hasn't beaten many rivals in his four official races (6f) and beaten in another trial only last week.
runners.comment: Hasn't beaten many rivals in his four official races and beaten in another trial last week

runners.name: Young Legacy
runners.spotlight: Third in a recent trial but hasn't managed better than tenth in four official races (6f/7f).
runners.comment: Third in a recent trial but hasn't managed better than tenth in four official races

runners.name: Call Me Success
runners.spotlight: Reserve; all three runs at 6f and delivered some good late work here this month, since when he's won a trial; has a future and probably over 7f.
runners.comment: Reserve; improved third over 6f here last time and has since won a trial

runners.name: Nyx Gluck
runners.spotlight: In a solid run of form at this track over 7f and was caught out by the drop to 6f last time; he's well in with Circuit Fiery on their runs here in February when they finished upsides; player.
runners.comment: He's well in with Circuit Fiery on their runs here in February when they finished upsides

runners.name: Best Farm
runners.spotlight: Ability in barrier trials and in his two races here, over 5f and 6f; wasn't beaten too far on either occasion and may have more to offer; draw could have been kinder, however.
runners.comment: Ability in barrier trials and in his two races here, over 5f and 6f; may have more to give

runners.name: Virtuous Phantom
runners.spotlight: Very opposable on his official runs (6f-1m) and recent trial form is nothing to shout about.
runners.comment: Very opposable on his official runs (6f-1m) and recent trial form nothing to shout about
runners.owner: Andrew Fan Chun Wah & Karson Choi Ka Tsan

runners.name: Family Knight
runners.spotlight: AW winner in Britain but no closer than 5l in four local runs for current connections.
runners.comment: AW winner in Britain but no closer than 5l in four local runs for current connections
runners.owner: Stephanie Fung Wing Man Kelly Fung Et Al

runners.name: State Security
runners.spotlight: Beaten in six trials since the turn of the year, managing no better than fourth; likely best watched on official debut.
runners.comment: Beaten in six trials since the turn of the year, managing no better than fourth
runners.owner: Marc Chan & Lily Chan Li Lei

runners.name: Circuit Fiery
runners.spotlight: Saw his consistency rewarded with a second career success gained over C&D four weeks ago when Zac Purton did the business for odds-on backers; that was comfortable from the front sohe  could deal with the 8lb hike; drawn low in his last three races but the win here in December was from stall 9.
runners.comment: Consistent 6yo who made all latest; could well be able to deal with the 8lb hike
runners.owner: Jason Cheung Cho Yee & Cheung Ka Shing

races.name: Graduate School Alumni Handicap (Class 4) (Course A+3) (Turf)

runners.name: Better And Better
runners.spotlight: Flopped when odds-on three starts ago but generally very consistent over 6f/7f this season and has slipped to a good mark; considered, despite wide draw.
runners.comment: Generally very consistent this season; considered, despite wide draw

runners.name: Runjeet
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all six starts and needs only to drop another 1lb to be eligible for a drop in grade.
runners.comment: Unplaced all six starts; will soon be eligible for Class 5 races

runners.name: Alabama Song
runners.spotlight: Won C&D novice last July but largely disappointing in handicaps this season; ran okay when sixth here a fortnight ago and is well drawn again but others appeal more.
runners.comment: Ran okay here a fortnight ago but has largely been disappointing this season

runners.name: Forerunner
runners.spotlight: Reserve; much more exposed than some of these but was very close second at Happy Valley this month and remains well handicapped after recent 2lb nudge.
runners.comment: Went very close at Happy Valley this month and remains on a good mark
runners.owner: Ng Tai Wing Anson Or Yiu Wah & Henry Yeung Mun Kin

runners.name: Live Wire
runners.spotlight: Perhaps a shade disappointing when only midfield here three weeks ago but made the frame on two of his first three starts and Zac Purton keeps the ride; may still have potential; must be considered.
runners.comment: Shade disappointing last time but probably still has potential; Zac Purton rides again

runners.name: Vigor Eye
runners.spotlight: Below form when favourite here last month but made the frame over C&D on his first three appearances and, from stall 1, will be a danger to all if back on song.
runners.comment: Below form last time but made the frame on first three starts and has stall 1 today

runners.name: Super Mastermind
runners.spotlight: Yet to show any significant promise after five starts (6f-7f) and seemingly needs a drop in grade.
runners.comment: Yet to show any significant promise after five starts

runners.name: Real Gentleman
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m on debut in Ireland last year but has struggled to get competitive in Hong Kong (five runs; 6f-1m).
runners.comment: Won on debut in Ireland but has struggled to get competitive in Hong Kong
runners.owner: Wong Chung Yin Wong Lai Ngor & Tracy Wong Chui

runners.name: Superb Boy
runners.spotlight: 0-8 for new stable this season; went close off today's mark over C&D in January and has obvious claims if judged on that performance but two down-the-field runs have followed.
runners.comment: Went close off this mark in January but two down-the-field runs have followed
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Mak Yiu Hay & Mak Kai Chung

runners.name: Joy Capital
runners.spotlight: Made all to cause 48-1 surprise over C&D in January, his second start, but safely held (albeit from wide stalls) twice since; others have more convincing profiles.
runners.comment: Twice safely held since surprise C&D win in January; others are more convincing
runners.owner: Jessica Man Yi Lee & Gregory Chi Ho Ho

runners.name: Lightning Ace
runners.spotlight: Made just a mildly encouraging debut when ninth at Happy Valley last month and is probably one for a bit further down the line.
runners.comment: Made just a mildly encouraging debut at Happy Valley last month

runners.name: Relentless Pursuit
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 3yo who kept on from the rear for creditable sixth over C&D from stall 14 on this month's debut; drawn wide again today but still enters calculations.
runners.comment: Kept on for creditable sixth from stall 14 here on this month's debut; a possible

runners.name: Warriors Dream
runners.spotlight: Came good for Brett Crawford at fourth attempt, with ready C&D success four weeks ago; 6lb higher today but could plausibly have more to offer for new stable.
runners.comment: Won readily over C&D this month his fourth stable start; one to consider
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Stephen Ho Wai Man F Ho Ka Chun & D Ho Ka Chung

runners.name: Prestige Emperor
runners.spotlight: Out of a prolific 5f-7.5f winner; recent trials have been promising and he's well drawn for this official debut; interesting to see how he fares.
runners.comment: Recent trials have been promising and he's well drawn for official debut

runners.name: Mighty Fighter
runners.spotlight: Out of a 5f winner; makes official debut after some encouraging trials and with Hugh Bowman a good booking but probably best watched all the same.
runners.comment: Makes debut after some encouraging trials but probably best watched

runners.name: Captain Link
runners.spotlight: Reserve; ex-Australian 4yo who is yet to make a splash in Hong Kong after five starts.
runners.comment: Ex-Australian 4yo who is yet to make a splash in Hong Kong after five starts

races.name: S.H. Ho Alumni Handicap (Class 3) (Course A+3) (Turf)

runners.name: Sunny Da Best
runners.spotlight: Posted sound effort when fourth over C&D three weeks ago and remains on a workable mark but is a lot more exposed than some of these, and not particularly reliable.
runners.comment: Ran quite well over C&D three weeks ago but is a lot more exposed than some of these

runners.name: Uranus Star
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m at Happy Valley in November, his second Hong Kong start, and has been placed over that C&D since but was below form on latest outing and this drop back to 7f may not be ideal.
runners.comment: 1m winner in November but below form last time and this drop to 7f is not ideal

runners.name: Brilliant Express
runners.spotlight: Won three times over C&D in early part of 2025 but has not been in the same form this season and managed only seventh behind Salon S here on latest outing.
runners.comment: Three C&D wins in early 2025 but not in same form this season

runners.name: California Waves
runners.spotlight: 0-9 in Hong Kong but belied 92-1 odds when third from a wide stall over C&D three weeks ago and has a much better draw today; could have a say.
runners.comment: Placed at big odds from wide stall over C&D this month and much better drawn today

runners.name: Romantic Gladiator
runners.spotlight: Looked promising when winning over 7f as a 2yo in Britain last July, and moves back up in trip after keeping on for fourth over 6f on last month's Hong Kong debut at Happy Valley; interesting.
runners.comment: Kept on for fourth over 6f on recent HK debut; this return to 7f will suit

runners.name: Lucky Doctor
runners.spotlight: Safely held on first two Hong Kong starts (both 6f) and difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Safely held on both Hong Kong starts; difficult to enthuse over
runners.owner: Dr Bosco Chan Dr Chu Wai Ming Et Al

runners.name: Ladys Love
runners.spotlight: Won over 5.5f on debut in Australia last August and made pretty good start to Hong Kong career when fifth here (6f) last month; up in trip again today; should have more to offer.
runners.comment: Won in Australia last year and made pretty good start to HK career last month
runners.owner: HK Association Of Lady Horse Lovers Syndicate

runners.name: Alls Well
runners.spotlight: Came from off the pace for emphatic C&D win last month and is now 2-10 in Hong Kong; moves up in grade today but evidently in very good nick; can't be ignored.
runners.comment: Emphatic C&D winner last month and now 2-10 in Hong Kong; up in grade today
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Alan Leung Wai Zen

runners.name: Salon S
runners.spotlight: Very promising 3yo with 3-3 strike-rate (6f-7f), having won stylishly from the front on each occasion; not ideally drawn today but it's very hard to gauge the limit of his ability.
runners.comment: Very promising 3yo with 3-3 strike-rate; limit of ability very hard to gauge
runners.owner: 24/25 Frankie Lor Fu Chuen Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: Do You Just
runners.spotlight: Began Hong Kong career with two disappointing 1m runs but his C&D fifth a fortnight ago was much more encouraging; he now needs to build on that effort.
runners.comment: Took big step forward when fifth over C&D this month, his third Hong Kong start

runners.name: Lucky Sam Gor
runners.spotlight: Four wins this season (three over C&D); encountered traffic when only fourth in latest handicap (1m) and subsequent down-the-field finish in Classic Cup (1m1f) is not viewed harshly; may yet have more improvement to come.
runners.comment: Four wins this season (three over C&D); further progress remains possible

runners.name: Sugar Goodson
runners.spotlight: Caused 33-1 surprise when winning 1m Irish maiden on debut in October; makes Hong Kong debut in a competitive race but good market support will bring him into the equation.
runners.comment: 33-1 winner of 1m Irish maiden in October; makes Hong Kong debut today

races.name: CW Chu Alumni Handicap (Class 2) (Course A+3) (Turf)

runners.name: Ladys Choice
runners.spotlight: Now 9lb lower than when going close over C&D on seasonal debut in October but his subsequent form has been underwhelming; drawn wide today and needs to up his game.
runners.comment: Went close on seasonal debut in October but subsequent form has been underwhelming
runners.owner: HK Association Of Lady Horse Lovers Syndicate

runners.name: Galactic Voyage
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who moves up in grade after two convincing C&D wins this year; probably still on the upgrade and can't be ignored.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 4yo who moves up in grade after two convincing C&D wins this year; a player
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Lam Ngai And Emily Lam Huen

runners.name: Magnifique
runners.spotlight: Very progressive 4yo who won on dirt here three weeks ago and now has 5-7 strike-rate; needs to prove he can transfer his latest portion of improvement back to turf but still taken very seriously.
runners.comment: Very progressive 4yo who won on dirt this month and now has 5-7 strike-rate
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Kirk Wong King Wai

runners.name: Young Champion
runners.spotlight: Placed on last three appearances, most recently when third at Happy Valley 11 days ago, but probably lacks the early zip to make full use of his inside draw today and others in this line-up have much more potential.
runners.comment: Placed on last three outings but others in this line-up have much more potential

runners.name: Victor The Winner
runners.spotlight: C&D Group 1 winner in 2024 but out of form this season, showing no real sign of a revival for a new stable this year.
runners.comment: C&D Group 1 winner in 2024 but out of form this season; revival needed

runners.name: Chateauneuf
runners.spotlight: Won with a bit left in the tank at Happy Valley in November and his subsequent 5f fifth here was perfectly respectable but not much has gone to plan since the turn of the year and he now has a point to prove.
runners.comment: Not much has gone to plan since the turn of the year and he now has a point to prove

runners.name: Bottomuptogether
runners.spotlight: Made very good progress last season but has not yet recaptured that form this term; absent since weakening into midfield over C&D in December and needs 99-day break to have done him good.
runners.comment: Yet to hit top gear this season and absent since midfield finish over C&D in December

runners.name: Bulb General
runners.spotlight: Began this season with two very comfortable C&D wins and is now 3-4 overall; upped in grade for this first run since October but could still have much more to offer.
runners.comment: Began season with two very comfortably C&D wins and remains lightly raced

runners.name: Patch Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner in early part of this season and probably posted another career-best effort when placed over C&D in January; nicely drawn today and a likely contender again.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this season; still in good form and well drawn today; likely contender

runners.name: Sky Trust
runners.spotlight: Posted career-best performance when winning narrowly over C&D last month and ran at least as well when second off new mark here three weeks ago; respected in current mood.
runners.comment: Career-best form when winning here last month and second off new mark three weeks ago
runners.owner: Bryan Kwan Hon Ming Jessica Kwan Mun Hang Et Al

races.name: Wu Yee Sun Alumni Handicap (Class 3) (Course A+3) (Turf)

runners.name: Geneva
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner who returned from 280-day layoff with pleasing third over C&D this month; remains on career-high mark but will be a tough nut to crack if that recent outing brought him on.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner who returned from layoff with pleasing C&D third this month

runners.name: Akermanis Gold
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only four Hong Kong starts and has shown some promise, most notably when fifth over C&D two runs ago; well drawn today but others have more pressing claims all the same.
runners.comment: Has shown some promise in Hong Kong but others have more pressing claims

runners.name: Rising Force
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who needed only to be pushed out to score over C&D from stall 14 in January and has finished second here twice since; likely contender again.
runners.comment: Runner-up twice since winning from wide stall here in January; big player again
runners.owner: Tai Woon Shing & Chan Koon Man

runners.name: Beauty Glory
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m at Happy Valley in November and went close over 6f there last month but this season's record is very mixed and he's not the percentage call here.
runners.comment: Has gone close over 6f since 1m win in November but has very mixed record

runners.name: Happy Index
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo with form-figures of 312 over C&D since arriving in Hong Kong; drawn wide today but probably still has potential and needs to be taken very seriously.
runners.comment: Ex-Australian 4yo with form-figures of 312 over C&D since arriving in Hong Kong

runners.name: Embrace Aberdeen
runners.spotlight: Improving 3yo who scored over C&D in first-time tongue-tie/cheekpieces last month and is now 2-4; upped in grade today but still features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Improving 3yo with 2-4 strike-rate; up in grade today but enters calculations

runners.name: Armour War Eagle
runners.spotlight: Made the frame five consecutive times over 6f on turf/dirt in January/February and didn't run badly from stall 10 here this month but has another wide draw today and will probably find others in this line-up better handicapped.
runners.comment: In good form this year but has wide draw and others look better handicapped

runners.name: Perfectday
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f winner; still lightly raced after only six starts but recent efforts suggest he needs to kick on again.
runners.comment: Dual 6f winner; still lightly raced but needs to kick on again
runners.owner: Choi Yui Hin & Daniel Choi Yui Fai

runners.name: Jubilant Winner
runners.spotlight: Three C&D wins this season, the latest in this grade last month; evidently in prime form; ought to make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Three C&D wins this season, the latest in this grade last month; respected

runners.name: Vulcanus
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f dirt winner in Class 4 this season but opposable based on recent turf form in this higher grade.
runners.comment: Dual 6f dirt winner in Class 4 this season; opposable on turf in this higher grade

runners.name: Double Money
runners.spotlight: Tailed off when 61-1 for Hong Kong debut at Happy Valley in January; can't be recommended.
runners.comment: Tailed off when 61-1 for Hong Kong debut at Happy Valley in January

runners.name: Grit Spirit
runners.spotlight: Won five-runner 5.5f maiden on debut in Australia last June but makes Hong Kong debut after a layoff and in a competitive race.
runners.comment: Won five-runner 5.5f maiden on debut in Australia last June; Hong Kong debut today

runners.name: Red Maestro
runners.spotlight: Created good impression when winning comfortably on debut in Ireland (6f, AW) in January 2025; makes Hong Kong debut after 14-month absence; market may guide.
runners.comment: Impressive on debut in Ireland; makes Hong Kong debut after 14-month absence
runners.owner: The Hon Ronald Arculli GBM GBS JP & Johanna K J Arculli BBS

races.name: Morningside And Lee Woo Sing Alumni Handicap (Class 3) (Course A+3) (Turf)

runners.name: Embraces
runners.spotlight: Yet to win this season but has posted a couple of good efforts (both over 1m) this year and continues to edge down the weights; might be the answer.
runners.comment: Has posted two decent efforts this year and continues to edge down the weights
runners.owner: Thomas Chung Yat Fan & Joe Ho Pui Lung

runners.name: Superb Kid
runners.spotlight: Belied triple-digit odds when running-on fifth over C&D last month, his third run for Me Tsui, but was safely held when dropped back to 7f three weeks ago; not the most obvious answer.
runners.comment: Ran well a big odds two starts ago but safely held since; look elsewhere

runners.name: Violet Star
runners.spotlight: Reserve; yet to be placed after four Hong Kong starts but was denied a clear run when fifth at Happy Valley recently; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Unplaced all four HK starts but was denied clear run when fifth at Happy Valley recently

runners.name: Another World
runners.spotlight: Gained narrow win on dirt here in December and has remained in good form on that surface since but wasn't seen to quite the same effect when back on turf this month.
runners.comment: In good form on dirt this season; not seen to same effect when back on turf

runners.name: Withallmyfaith
runners.spotlight: Reserve; front-runner; placed over 1m on three of his six starts since blinkers were added in November and will have each-way claims again if he gets a run today.
runners.comment: Front-runner; placed on three of six starts since blinkers were added

runners.name: Chill Kaka
runners.spotlight: Seemingly back in good form when close second at Happy Valley in December but three lesser efforts have followed this year and he can't be supported with much confidence.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with a good run but three lesser efforts have followed this year

runners.name: The Golden Knight
runners.spotlight: Awarded Class 4 C&D contest after finishing close second to Master Trillion three weeks ago, opening his account at the ninth attempt; combination of 6lb rise and rise in grade asks quite a lot of him, though.
runners.comment: Awarded Class 4 C&D contest this month; 6lb higher in a higher grade today
runners.owner: Wilson Ngan Ping Shing Katherine Ngan Mei Sze Et Al

runners.name: Master Trillion
runners.spotlight: Emerged from spell in the doldrums with two good recent efforts, more recently when finishing first (demoted to second) over C&D; recent 2lb rise enforces this move back up in grade but he remains well handicapped on older form; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Back in good nick in recent weeks and remains well handicapped on older form

runners.name: Rising Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Dual 7f Class 4 winner this season but has found things tougher since upped to this grade and is yet fully to convince over 1m.
runners.comment: Dual 7f Class 4 winner this season but finding things tougher in this grade
runners.owner: James Kwan Wai Ming & Leung Kam Chuen

runners.name: China Win
runners.spotlight: In-form 4yo who won twice over 1m1f (once here) in January and lost out only by a neck over that trip at Happy Valley this month; deserted by Zac Purton today but still likely to be on the premises again.
runners.comment: Won twice over 1m1f in January and went close over that trip this month

runners.name: Endued
runners.spotlight: Consistent and steadily progressive 4yo who held on well for a narrow success three weeks ago and has now won twice over C&D this season; recent 6lb rise demands more from him, though.
runners.comment: Held on narrowly three weeks ago and has now won twice over C&D this season

runners.name: The Boom Box
runners.spotlight: Kept on for very respectable fifth over C&D this month and is now only 3lb higher than when winning at Happy Valley (1m) in November but others have stronger claims.
runners.comment: Very respectable fifth over C&D this month but others have stronger claims

runners.name: Family Jewel
runners.spotlight: Unable to land a telling blow on this month's belated reappearance outing and remains 5lb above last season's winning mark; might need another run or two before he's of interest.
runners.comment: Safely held on this month's belated seasonal debut; may need another run or two
runners.owner: Tony Stradmoor Billy Li Sze Kuen Et Al

runners.name: Morgan Success
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a C&D win but has finished well down the field on all four appearances this term; needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: Well down the field on all four appearances this season

runners.name: Glittering Legend
runners.spotlight: Smart when based in Britain; began Hong Kong career with two down-the-field runs but this month's C&D fourth was a pleasing step forward; contender if able to build on that recent effort.
runners.comment: Made low-key start to HK career but recent C&D fourth was much better

runners.name: Lovero
runners.spotlight: Has form-figures of 2123 over C&D for new stable since December; drawn wide today but remains well handicapped on pick of last season's form and Zac Purton takes the ride; respected.
runners.comment: In good form over C&D for new stable and remains on a good mark; respected

races.name: Takamatsunomiya Kinen (Grade 1) (Turf)
Prix Edmond Blanc (Group 3) (Turf)

runners.name: Native Warrior
runners.spotlight: Rediscovered his best when a visor was added last June, winning three handicaps from five starts in them, the last being the valuable Challenge Cup (7f, soft) at Ascot; ran well in the Balmoral Handicap (1m, good) when last seen under a penalty and looks ready for a try in Group company, with blinkers going on for first time; stays a mile and seems to handle most ground.
runners.comment: Rediscovered his best when a visor was added last year, and now in first-time blinkers

runners.name: Silius
runners.spotlight: Record of seven wins from 16 starts shows how talented he is and the 4yo heads into this attempting a hat-trick, his most recent win coming over C&D on very soft, when Instant Fragile, Narkez and Trust On were at least 4.5l behind; looks the one to beat.
runners.comment: Beat Instant Fragile, Narkez and Trust On last time and looks the one to beat

runners.name: Un Instant
runners.spotlight: Won six of his 25 starts, but only one time in Listed company, and his sole start at Group level last summer was modest; has a race-fitness edge on some of these but makes limited appeal for the win.
runners.comment: Has a race-fitness edge on some of these but makes limited appeal for the win

runners.name: Trust On
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent at the start of his career but did become progressive at the end of last season, notably beating Moon Cloud in a C&D conditions event and then Silius and Narkez in a C&D Listed contest on heavy ground; didn't confirm that form, again over C&D, on his seasonal return, with Instant Fragile also ahead of him, but clearly a player if improving from that effort.
runners.comment: Became progressive at the end of last season but was behind a few of these 24 days ago

runners.name: Skukuza
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a fine season in 2025, landing a valuable handicap at the Curragh (1m, good) as well as a Listed race over this distance at the same track; ran well in defeat after, including when ahead of Dreamliner in the Prix Daniel Wildenstein (1m, very soft) and should have benefitted for the outing at Doha in February when Christophe Soumillon took the ride; probably wants good going ideally but his run at Longchamp last October shows he handles some ease in the ground.
runners.comment: Should have benefitted for the run at Doha in Feb when Christophe Soumillon took the ride

runners.name: Moon Cloud
runners.spotlight: Unexposed filly, considering this will be only her fifth run, from a stable to be respected; only ever raced over this C&D, winning two times, with one of the defeats a narrow one to Trust On; could easily take a bit step forward this season.
runners.comment: Unexposed filly, who has only ever raced over this C&D; interesting

runners.name: Dreamliner
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick last season over a mile, including a career-best in a Deauville Group 3 but then couldn't match that level in the Group 2 Prix Daniel Wildenstein, when behind Skukuza while receiving weight for age; trip and ground are fine and this does look an ideal starting point for the season, but a bit of work to do with the Ed Dunlop runner.
runners.comment: Completed a hat-trick last season over a mile, but a bit of work to do with Skukuza

runners.name: Narkez
runners.spotlight: Produced some smart efforts as a 3yo and 4yo, winning a C&D handicap last year following a gelding operation, this after a winning seasonal return at Longchamp 169 days earlier; held later in the campaign, though, and was behind both Silius and Instant Fragile in a Listed race over C&D earlier in the month; others make stronger appeal.
runners.comment: Behind both Silius and Instant Fragile in a Listed race over C&D earlier in the month

runners.name: Instant Fragile
runners.spotlight: Run in Listed company on seven of his eight starts, winning one over a mile and another at 1m2f; fine second on debut for Gerald Mosse behind Silius over C&D as a 25-1 shot, when ahead of Narkkez and Trust On, and while he needs a further step up, the 4yo is lightly raced compared to some of these, so may have improvement in him.
runners.comment: Fine second on debut for Gerald Mosse behind Silius over C&D, and may have more to come

runners.name: Sparks Fly
runners.spotlight: Another in the field with a fine strike-rate and ended last season with a career-best over this C&D, on very soft, making most of the running to land a Group 3, a fourth C&D success in all; conditions are likely to be in her favour and is to be respected but has a mixed record after a break, so will need to return at her best.
runners.comment: Loves this C&D and is a player if ready to go after a 154-day break
